Technical Specifications Parameter Value Brand Name CooperVision Proclear® 1-Day Manufacturer CooperVision Material Omafilcon A (PC Hydrogel) Material Technology PC Technology™ (Phosphorylcholine) Water Content 60% Oxygen Transmissibility (Dk/t) 28 (at –3.00 D) Lens Design Aspheric — enhanced clarity & aberration correction Base Curve (BC) 8.7 mm Diameter (DIA) 14.2 mm Centre Thickness 0.09 mm (at –3.00 D) UV Protection None Handling Tint Yes (light visibility tint) Power Range (SPH) –12.00 to +8.00 D Astigmatism Correction No (spherical; see Proclear 1-Day for Astigmatism) Modality Daily disposable — single use, discard after each day Extended / Overnight Wear Not  — remove before sleep Pack Contents 90 Lenses FDA Claim Cleared for improved comfort in dry eye lens wearers Key Technology: PC Technology™ & Omafilcon A PC Technology™ PC Technology™ is CooperVision's proprietary material system incorporating phosphorylcholine (PC) molecules — the same type of molecule found naturally in the outer layer of healthy human cell membranes. These PC molecules are inherently water-loving: they attract water from the surrounding tear film and bind it to the lens surface, creating a sustained moisture shield that persists throughout the wearing day. Unlike surface coatings that are deposited on top of the lens and can wear away with blinking, the PC molecules are embedded within the Omafilcon A polymer itself — meaning the moisture-attracting property is permanent and consistent from first insertion to final removal. Omafilcon A · 60% H₂O Omafilcon A is the hydrogel material produced by CooperVision's PC Technology process. Its 60% water content — higher than most daily disposable hydrogels — contributes to the lens's soft, naturally moist feel on the eye. The material is biocompatible: it resists protein and lipid deposit accumulation, maintains its shape and optical properties throughout the wearing day, and remains 96% hydrated after 12 or more hours of continuous wear in clinical testing. The thin centre thickness of 0.09 mm contributes to the lens's comfortable, low-awareness feel from first insertion. Aspheric Optical Design Standard spherical contact lenses introduce spherical aberration — optical distortion that causes halos, glare, and reduced visual contrast, especially noticeable in dim lighting. Proclear 1-Day's aspheric design corrects this aberration by progressively flattening the lens curvature from centre to periphery, maintaining sharp focus quality across the full optical zone. The result is clearer, higher-contrast vision in all lighting conditions — an advantage that extends the lens's comfort and performance  from hydration to optical clarity. The combination of PC Technology's moisture-binding PC molecules, Omafilcon A's biocompatible high-water hydrogel, and the aspheric optical design explains why Proclear 1-Day consistently performs at the top of wearer comfort surveys — and why it holds the unique distinction of being the only daily disposable lens with FDA  for an improved comfort claim in dry eye lens wearers. Who Is Proclear 1-Day 90 Pack For? Proclear 1-Day was specifically engineered for wearers whose primary requirement from a daily lens is sustained comfort — particularly those who have experienced lens-related dryness, irritation, or end-of-day discomfort with other daily or monthly lenses. Its FDA comfort claim and 96% hydration retention make it uniquely suited to the following wearer profiles. 💧 Dry Eye & Comfort-Sensitive Wearers The FDA-cleared dry eye comfort claim is specific to Proclear lenses and no other daily disposable. PC Technology's phosphorylcholine moisture shield directly addresses the mechanism of lens-related dryness — maintaining 96% hydration at 12 hours — making Proclear 1-Day the clinically supported choice for wearers with lens-induced dryness symptoms. 💻 Screen-Heavy Professionals Prolonged screen use reduces blink rate significantly, accelerating tear film evaporation and lens surface drying. PC Technology's water-binding mechanism actively counteracts this process — maintaining lens hydration through long screen-based working days without requiring rewetting drops or lens removal. 🌬️ Air-Conditioned & Low-Humidity Environments Air conditioning, heating, and low-humidity environments accelerate lens dehydration. The PC molecules' continuous water-attracting action from the surrounding tear film is particularly effective in these conditions — sustaining comfort in environments where standard daily lenses become noticeably uncomfortable within hours. 🌙 Long-Day Wearers 96% hydration retained after 12+ hours means Proclear 1-Day is designed for extended daily wearing schedules. For wearers whose day regularly extends beyond 10–12 hours of lens wear, the sustained moisture performance of PC Technology provides consistent comfort that many competing daily hydrogels cannot maintain. 🧼 Hygiene-First & Allergy Wearers Daily replacement eliminates deposit accumulation, cleaning solution interactions, and case contamination entirely. For allergy sufferers — who are particularly susceptible to lens surface deposits triggering ocular reactions — Proclear 1-Day's biocompatible PC material and daily format are a clinically sound combination. 📋 Wide  Range SPH –12.00 to +8.00 D in 0.25 D steps (0.50 D steps at the extremes) covers a broad spectrum of myopic and hyperopic prescriptions, including higher powers that many daily lenses do not accommodate. The 90-pack provides a full 3-month supply per eye at a lower per-lens cost than the 30-pack equivalent. Daily Wear Guide — Proclear 1-Day As a daily disposable lens, Proclear 1-Day requires no cleaning or storage routine. Each lens is worn for one day and discarded. The following steps apply to every wearing day. 1Wash and dry your hands before every lens handling session. Use soap and water, then dry with a clean lint-free towel. Clean hands are essential — even a daily disposable lens can introduce pathogens if handled without proper hygiene. 2Open the blister carefully and inspect the lens. Check for tears or defects. The light handling tint makes the lens easy to see on your fingertip. Confirm the lens is right-side out — it should form a smooth, even bowl shape with edges curving inward. 3Insert gently and blink to centre. Place the lens on the cornea with lids held open, and blink normally to centre it. Proclear 1-Day's thin, soft Omafilcon A material adapts quickly and comfortably to the ocular surface — most wearers report immediate lens comfort from first insertion. 4Wear for one day only. Proclear 1-Day is a single-use daily disposable — each lens is designed, tested, and  for one day of wear. Never reuse, rinse and reinsert, or store a used lens. Remove and discard at the end of every wearing day without exception, regardless of comfort level. 5Remove before sleep and water exposure. Daily wear lenses are not  for overnight use. Always remove before sleeping, napping, swimming, or showering. All water sources carry micro-organisms that can cause serious eye infections on a contact lens surface. 6Discard after removal. Once removed, the lens is finished — dispose of it immediately. Each sealed blister pack provides one sterile, fresh lens for each new day, eliminating the deposit accumulation, cleaning chemistry, and case contamination risks of reusable lenses entirely. 💡 Dry eye tip: If you experience persistent dryness or discomfort even with Proclear 1-Day, this may indicate a clinical dry eye condition that requires assessment beyond a lens change. Proclear 1-Day is cleared for wearers with mild lens-related dryness symptoms — moderate to severe dry eye disease requires evaluation by an eye care professional who can recommend appropriate management alongside or instead of contact lens wear. Proclear 1-Day carries FDA  for the claim that it may provide improved comfort for contact lens wearers who experience mild discomfort or symptoms relating to dryness during lens wear. This is a specific, regulated clinical claim — not general marketing language. No other daily disposable contact lens holds this FDA . It reflects the clinical evidence supporting PC Technology's effectiveness in maintaining hydration and reducing lens-related dryness symptoms. It applies to mild lens-induced dryness; wearers with diagnosed dry eye disease should consult their eye care professional for personalised management. How long does one Proclear 1-Day 90 Pack last? One 90-lens box provides a 3-month supply for one eye, based on daily wear. For both eyes, two boxes — one per eye — give a full 3-month binocular supply. Two 90-packs per order is the standard purchase for full both-eye coverage for 3 months. Can I reuse Proclear 1-Day lenses? No. Proclear 1-Day is a daily disposable lens  and designed for one day of wear only. Reusing the lens — even after rinsing — introduces deposit accumulation, microbial contamination, and infection risk. The PC Technology's moisture performance is also optimised for single-day use. Remove and discard each lens at the end of every wearing day without exception. Does Proclear 1-Day have UV protection? No. Proclear 1-Day does not include UV-blocking capability. If UV protection is important for your lifestyle, discuss options with your eye care professional. Note that no contact lens provides complete UV protection — UV-blocking sunglasses should always be worn alongside any contact lens for full outdoor UV protection. Can Proclear 1-Day correct astigmatism or presbyopia? The standard Proclear 1-Day spherical lens corrects myopia and hyperopia only. CooperVision also offers Proclear 1-Day for Astigmatism (toric daily) and Proclear 1-Day Multifocal (daily presbyopia lens), both using the same PC Technology platform. All variants are available at contactlens365.com. Is Proclear 1-Day a silicone hydrogel lens? No. Proclear 1-Day is made from Omafilcon A — a conventional hydrogel (PC Hydrogel), not a silicone hydrogel. Its Dk/t of 28 is lower than silicone hydrogel daily lenses, which typically range from 42 to 100+ Dk/t. However, for daily wear lenses worn for single days, clinical research indicates that a Dk/t of 24 or above is generally sufficient to maintain corneal health during daily wear. The distinctive advantage of Proclear 1-Day is not oxygen performance but its unique PC Technology moisture-binding system and FDA dry eye comfort claim. What power range is available in Proclear 1-Day? Proclear 1-Day is available from –12.00 D to +8.00 D. Powers from –6.00 to +5.00 D are available in 0.25 D steps. Powers beyond these values are available in 0.50 D steps. This is a notably wide range that includes many higher powers not available in competing daily disposable lenses. What is the current price for Proclear 1-Day 90 Pack? For current pricing and any active promotions, visit the Proclear 1-Day 90 Pack product page at contactlens365.com. } .tech-row { flex-direction: column; } } CooperVision · MyDay® contactlens365.com MyDay® ToricDaily 30 Pack CooperVision's premium daily disposable toric contact lens for astigmatism — Smart Silicone™ Stenfilcon A material with Aquaform® Technology for all-day comfort, Optimized Toric Lens Geometry™ for predictable axis stability, Dk/t 80, and built-in UVA/UVB protection. 30 lenses per box. ✦ Daily Toric For Astigmatism 30 Lenses / Box Aquaform® Technology Smart Silicone™ · 54% H₂O UVA/UVB Protection DailyDisposable 80Dk/t O₂ Transmit. 54%Water Content 4CYL Powers ✓ 100% Genuine 📅 Expiry Date Checked 🚚 Fast Shipping 🔒 Secure Checkout ↩ Easy Returns MyDay Toric Daily Disposable 30 Pack — Daily Contact Lens for Astigmatism The CooperVision MyDay® Toric Daily Disposable 30 Pack is a premium silicone hydrogel daily toric contact lens designed for wearers who demand both exceptional astigmatism correction and all-day comfort in a single-use format. Built on CooperVision's Smart Silicone™ Stenfilcon A material with Aquaform® Technology, the MyDay Toric delivers the oxygen performance and natural wettability of a high-end silicone hydrogel — without surface treatments or added wetting agents — combined with the convenience and hygiene of daily replacement. Order MyDay Toric online at contactlens365.com with 100%  authenticity and fast same-day dispatch across all available parameters. What distinguishes the MyDay Toric from conventional daily toric lenses is the integration of three complementary technologies. Smart Silicone™ chemistry uses longer silicone chain molecules that transfer oxygen more efficiently while permanently bonding hydrophilic groups directly into the silicone matrix — giving the lens its natural wettability without relying on surface coatings that degrade during wear. Aquaform® Technology locks water into the material to maintain consistent comfort throughout the wearing day. And Optimized Toric Lens Geometry™ — featuring a uniform horizontal thickness profile and a wide ballast band — keeps the lens correctly oriented on the eye for consistent, predictable astigmatism correction from first blink to last removal. 🎯 Optimized Toric Lens Geometry™: A specially engineered wide ballast band and uniform horizontal thickness rapidly orient the MyDay Toric to the correct axis after insertion and maintain that orientation throughout the wearing day — delivering stable, consistent astigmatism correction in every  and every condition. The MyDay Toric covers a broad  range — SPH –10.00 to +6.00 D, four cylinder powers from –0.75 to –2.25 D, and axes in 10° steps across the full 180° — accommodating the majority of astigmatic prescriptions in a daily disposable silicone hydrogel. All available parameters are stocked at contactlens365.com for prompt dispatch. Technical Specifications Parameter Value Brand Name CooperVision MyDay® Toric Daily Disposable Manufacturer CooperVision Material Stenfilcon A (Silicone Hydrogel) Material Technology Smart Silicone™ Chemistry · Aquaform® Technology Water Content 54% Oxygen Transmissibility (Dk/t) 80 (at –3.00 D) Lens Design Toric — Optimized Toric Lens Geometry™ Base Curve (BC) 8.6 mm Diameter (DIA) 14.5 mm UV Protection Yes — UVA & UVB (Class 2: ~96% UVB, ~85% UVA) Handling Tint Yes (light visibility tint) Power Range (SPH) –10.00 to +6.00 D (0.25 D steps) Cylinder (CYL) –0.75 / –1.25 / –1.75 / –2.25 D Axis 10° to 180° in 10° steps (18 options) Modality Daily disposable — single use, discard after each day Extended / Overnight Wear Not  — remove before sleep Pack Contents 30 Lenses Key Technology: Smart Silicone™, Aquaform® & Optimized Toric Lens Geometry™ Smart Silicone™ Chemistry Most silicone hydrogel lenses require plasma surface treatments or added wetting agents to overcome silicone's natural hydrophobicity. MyDay's Smart Silicone™ chemistry takes a different approach — it uses longer silicone chain molecules that provide more efficient oxygen transfer, while permanently bonding hydrophilic groups directly within the silicone molecule itself. The result is a naturally wettable material that maintains its comfort properties throughout the wearing day without any surface  that can degrade or deplete during wear. Aquaform® Technology CooperVision's Aquaform® Technology creates an optimal balance between oxygen permeability and water retention in the Stenfilcon A material. It locks water molecules into the silicone hydrogel matrix, maintaining the lens's 54% water content consistently across a full day of wear. The same technology is used in CooperVision's Biofinity® monthly toric — bringing that platform's proven comfort  into a daily disposable format. This means consistent moisture, consistently clear eyes, and consistently comfortable wear from first lens in the morning to last lens out at night. Optimized Toric Lens Geometry™ Toric lenses must stay correctly oriented on the eye for the cylindrical correction to work — any significant rotation shifts the axis away from the intended correction and degrades vision quality. MyDay Toric's Optimized Toric Lens Geometry™ uses a uniform horizontal thickness profile combined with a wide ballast band to rapidly orient the lens after insertion and resist rotational displacement during blinking and eye movement. The design provides predictable, stable axis orientation across the full range of available cylinder powers and axes — from standard to extended-range prescriptions. These three technologies work in concert: Smart Silicone™ ensures oxygen reaches the cornea without compromising wettability; Aquaform® Technology locks in water for all-day comfort; and Optimized Toric Lens Geometry™ keeps the cylindrical correction precisely positioned. Together they define why the MyDay Toric consistently ranks among the highest-rated daily toric contact lenses for astigmatism in independent wearer assessments. Who Is the MyDay Toric Daily Disposable For? The MyDay Toric daily lens is designed for astigmatic wearers who want the highest level of daily disposable performance — premium material, proven toric stability, and the hygiene of a fresh lens every day. The following profiles benefit most. 🎯 Astigmatism — All Common Prescriptions SPH –10.00 to +6.00 D with four CYL powers (–0.75 to –2.25 D) and 18 axis options in 10° steps — covering with-the-rule, against-the-rule, and oblique astigmatism across the full spectrum of common prescriptions. 💧 Comfort-First Daily Wearers Aquaform® Technology and Smart Silicone™ chemistry work together to maintain the lens's 54% water content and natural surface wettability from morning to evening — without the progressive comfort decline seen in daily torics that rely on surface coatings or wetting drops. 💨 High-Oxygen Demand Wearers At Dk/t 80, MyDay Toric delivers substantially more oxygen to the cornea than conventional hydrogel daily torics, supporting healthy, clear, white eyes even during long wearing days. Particularly beneficial for wearers whose previous daily torics left their eyes looking or feeling tired by afternoon. 💻 Screen-Intensive Professionals Prolonged screen use reduces blink rate and accelerates lens surface drying. Smart Silicone's inherent wettability — not dependent on a surface coating — means the MyDay Toric remains consistently comfortable through long desk-based or digital work sessions without intervention. 🏃 Active & Sport Wearers The Optimized Toric Lens Geometry's wide ballast band and uniform horizontal thickness provide toric stability under the dynamic conditions of sport and physical activity — where lens movement and blinking frequency increase the demands on rotational stability. 🧼 Hygiene-Priority Wearers Daily replacement eliminates cleaning, storage, and case maintenance entirely. Each lens comes from a sealed blister pack — sterile, fresh, deposit-free. For astigmatic wearers who have experienced discomfort or infection with monthly toric lenses, the daily format represents a meaningful hygiene upgrade. The MyDay® Daily Disposable Lens Family MyDay Toric is part of CooperVision's MyDay daily disposable range — a family of premium silicone hydrogel daily lenses all built on the Smart Silicone™ Stenfilcon A platform with Aquaform® Technology. Daily Wear Guide — MyDay Toric As a daily disposable toric lens, MyDay Toric requires no cleaning or storage. Each lens is worn for one day and discarded. The following steps ensure safe, comfortable wear and consistent astigmatism correction throughout each wearing day. 1Wash and dry your hands before every lens handling session. Use soap and water, then dry with a clean lint-free towel. Clean hands are the single most important step in preventing lens-related eye infection. 2Open the blister carefully and inspect the lens before insertion. Check for tears or defects. The handling tint makes the lens easy to see on your fingertip. Confirm the lens forms a smooth bowl shape — edges curving inward, not flared outward. 3Insert and allow the toric to orient. Place the lens on the cornea, hold your lids open, and blink normally. The Optimized Toric Lens Geometry™ is designed to settle to the correct axis quickly — allow a few blinks before evaluating vision quality. Brief initial blur during orientation is normal. 4Allow a short settling period. If vision is slightly blurry immediately after insertion, blink normally and wait — the toric geometry will orient within seconds. If blurring persists beyond this initial settling, remove the lens, inspect it, and reinsert. Persistent vision disturbance should be discussed with your eye care professional. 5Wear for one day only. MyDay Toric is a single-use daily disposable — each lens is designed for one day of wear only. Never reuse, rinse and reinsert, or store a used lens. Remove and discard at the end of every wearing day without exception. 6Remove before sleep and water exposure. Daily wear lenses are not  for overnight use. Remove before sleeping, napping, swimming, or showering. All water sources carry micro-organisms that can cause serious eye infections on a contact lens surface. 7Discard after removal. Once removed, the lens is finished — discard immediately. A fresh lens from a new blister pack begins every day, eliminating deposit accumulation and contamination risks entirely. 💡 Toric stability tip: If you notice fluctuating or blurred vision during the day with MyDay Toric, this may indicate lens rotation — where the axis has shifted from its correct orientation. Blink firmly a few times to allow the Optimized Toric Lens Geometry™ to reorient. If the problem persists consistently across multiple lenses, consult your eye care professional — persistent rotation may indicate a fitting or  adjustment is needed. Frequently Asked Questions — MyDay Toric Daily Disposable 30 Pack What is Smart Silicone™ chemistry and why does it matter? Smart Silicone™ is CooperVision's proprietary silicone hydrogel chemistry used in Stenfilcon A. Unlike conventional silicone hydrogels that require plasma surface treatments to become wettable, Smart Silicone uses longer silicone chain molecules for more efficient oxygen transfer, and permanently bonds hydrophilic groups within the silicone molecule itself. This makes the lens surface naturally wettable without any coating that can wear away during the wearing day. The practical result is a lens that feels consistently comfortable from insertion to removal — not just in the morning. What is Optimized Toric Lens Geometry™? Optimized Toric Lens Geometry™ is CooperVision's toric stabilisation design used across their silicone hydrogel toric range, including the MyDay Toric. It combines a uniform horizontal thickness profile with a wide ballast band — a thicker lower portion of the lens that uses the weight and pressure of the eyelid to orient and stabilise the lens at the correct cylindrical axis. The geometry is designed to position the lens predictably after insertion and resist rotation during blinking and eye movement, providing stable, consistent astigmatism correction throughout the wearing day. How long does one MyDay Toric Daily Disposable 30 Pack last? Each lens is used for one day and discarded. One 30-lens box provides a 1-month supply for one eye, based on daily wear. For both eyes, two boxes — one per eye — give a full 1-month binocular supply. For regular full-time wearers of both eyes, the MyDay Toric 90 Pack offers a lower per-lens cost and 3 months of supply per eye in a single order. My vision is slightly blurry right after inserting MyDay Toric — is this normal? Yes, brief blur immediately after insertion is normal for toric lenses. The Optimized Toric Lens Geometry™ needs a few blinks to orient the lens to the correct cylindrical axis. This typically resolves within seconds. If blurring persists, blink firmly a few more times. If clear vision is not achieved within about 30 seconds, remove and reinsert the lens. Persistent vision instability throughout the wearing day should be discussed with your eye care professional — it may indicate lens rotation requiring a fitting review. What cylinder powers are available in MyDay Toric? MyDay Toric is available in four cylinder powers: –0.75 D, –1.25 D, –1.75 D, and –2.25 D. Axes are available in 10° increments from 10° to 180° — 18 axis positions in total. Spherical power range is –10.00 to +6.00 D in 0.25 D steps. Always order exactly as prescribed by your eye care professional. Does MyDay Toric have UV protection? Yes. MyDay Toric includes Class 2 UV protection — blocking approximately 96% of UVB rays and 85% of UVA rays. As with all UV-blocking contact lenses, this does not cover the entire eye and surrounding area, and should not replace UV-protective sunglasses for outdoor activities. The UV protection is a supplementary layer of defence, not a substitute for eyewear. Can I reuse MyDay Toric lenses or wear them for more than one day? No. MyDay Toric is a daily disposable lens — each lens is  and designed for exactly one day of wear. Reusing the lens introduces deposit accumulation, microbial contamination risk, and potential corneal hypoxia, regardless of how comfortable the lens feels. Remove and discard after each day's wear without exception. How does MyDay Toric compare to MyDay Toric 90 Pack? The lens inside both packs is identical — same Stenfilcon A material, same Smart Silicone™ chemistry, same Aquaform® Technology, same Optimized Toric Lens Geometry™. The 30-pack provides a 1-month supply per eye and is ideal for first-time users or part-time wearers. The 90-pack provides a 3-month supply per eye at a lower per-lens cost, making it the more economical choice for established daily wearers. Both are available at contactlens365.com. What is the current price for MyDay Toric Daily Disposable 30 Pack? For current pricing and any active promotions, visit the MyDay Toric Daily Disposable 30 Pack product page at contactlens365.com. } .tech-row { flex-direction: column; } } Bausch + Lomb · Biotrue contactlens365.com Biotrue® ONEday30 Pack Bausch + Lomb's bio-inspired daily disposable contact lens — HyperGel™ material with 78% water content matching the cornea's own biology, patented dehydration barrier retaining moisture for up to 16 hours, High Definition Optics, and built-in UVA/UVB protection. 30 lenses per box — a full 3-month supply for one eye. ✦ Daily Disposable 30 Lenses / Box HyperGel™ · 78% H₂O HD Optics · Aspheric UVA/UVB Protection BC 8.6 mm · Dia 14.2 mm DailyDisposable 78%Water Content 16hMoisture Retention 30Lenses / Box ✓ 100% Genuine 📅 Expiry Date Checked 🚚 Fast Shipping 🔒 Secure Checkout ↩ Easy Returns Biotrue ONEday 30 Pack — Daily Disposable Contact Lens The Bausch + Lomb Biotrue® ONEday 30 Pack is a daily disposable contact lens built around a single design philosophy: working with the eye's own biology rather than against it. Where most contact lenses are engineered as dry polymer materials and then treated to improve wettability, Biotrue ONEday starts from the opposite direction — its HyperGel™ material is bio-inspired from the ground up, with a 78% water content that precisely matches the natural water content of the human cornea, and a surface architecture that mimics the lipid layer of the tear film. The result is a daily lens that feels remarkably natural on the eye — and stays that way for up to 16 hours. Order the Biotrue ONEday 30 Pack online at contactlens365.com with 100%  authenticity and fast same-day dispatch. The 30-pack is the standard entry format for Biotrue ONEday — each box contains 30 lenses, providing a 1-month supply for one eye or a 15-day supply for both eyes together. It is the ideal pack size for wearers who are trying Biotrue ONEday for the first time, for those who wear lenses on a part-time basis, or for anyone who prefers to order more frequently rather than holding a larger stock. For regular daily wearers of both eyes, the 90-pack delivers a lower per-lens cost and 3 months of supply in a single order — see the Biotrue ONEday 90 Pack at contactlens365.com. All available powers are stocked across the full range from –12.00 to +6.00 D for prompt same-day dispatch. 💧 78% Water Content — Matched to the Cornea: HyperGel™ material contains the same proportion of water as the human cornea itself. Combined with the patented dehydration barrier, Biotrue ONEday retains approximately 98% of its moisture for up to 16 hours of wear — consistently comfortable from first insertion to end of day. Technical Specifications Parameter Value Brand Name Biotrue® ONEday Manufacturer Bausch + Lomb Material Nesofilcon A (HyperGel™) Material Technology Surface Active Technology™ · Patented Dehydration Barrier Water Content 78% Oxygen Transmissibility (Dk/t) 42 (at –3.00 D) Lens Design Aspheric — High Definition™ Optics Base Curve (BC) 8.6 mm Diameter (DIA) 14.2 mm UV Protection Yes — UVA & UVB blocking Handling Tint Yes (light blue visibility tint) Power Range (SPH) –12.00 to +6.00 D (0.25 D steps) Astigmatism Correction No (spherical; see Biotrue ONEday for Astigmatism) Modality Daily disposable — single use, discard after each day Extended / Overnight Wear Not  — remove before sleep Pack Contents 30 Lenses Key Technology: HyperGel™, Dehydration Barrier & HD Optics HyperGel™ · Nesofilcon A HyperGel is Bausch + Lomb's bio-inspired lens material — a unique hydrogel formulation that achieves 78% water content, precisely matching the natural water content of the human cornea. Unlike conventional hydrogels or silicone hydrogels, HyperGel incorporates Surface Active Technology™, which orients the hydrophilic component of the material towards the lens surface. This creates an inherently wettable outer surface that mimics the lipid layer of the natural tear film — reducing dehydration and maintaining the lens's moist, comfortable character throughout the wearing day without relying on added surface coatings that deplete over time. Patented Dehydration Barrier The most common cause of daily lens discomfort is progressive dehydration — as the wearing day extends, lenses lose water to the environment and to the tear film, becoming stiffer and less comfortable. Biotrue ONEday's patented dehydration barrier is built into the Nesofilcon A polymer structure itself, actively resisting water loss from the lens matrix. In Bausch + Lomb studies, this results in moisture retention of approximately 98% after 16 hours of wear — a figure that far exceeds most competing daily disposable lenses and explains why Biotrue ONEday wearers consistently report all-day comfort even in dry or air-conditioned environments. High Definition™ Optics · Aspheric Biotrue ONEday incorporates an aspheric optical design that corrects spherical aberration — the distortion introduced by standard spherical lens designs that causes halos, glare, and reduced contrast sensitivity. The aspheric profile progressively flattens from the optical centre to the lens periphery, maintaining sharp focus and high contrast across the full optical zone. The result is notably crisp, clear vision in all lighting conditions — including low light situations where spherical aberration is most visually disruptive. The combination of HyperGel's cornea-matched water content, the patented dehydration barrier, and the aspheric HD Optics design makes Biotrue ONEday one of the most comprehensively engineered daily disposables in Bausch + Lomb's range. It delivers comfort and vision performance typically associated with premium lenses, within the hygiene and convenience of a daily replacement schedule. Who Is the 30 Pack Right For? Biotrue ONEday is available in 30-lens and 90-lens boxes. The 30-pack is the right choice in three specific situations — and understanding when to choose it over the 90-pack helps you get the most from your lens purchase. 🧪 First-Time Biotrue ONEday Wearers If you are trying Biotrue ONEday for the first time — either as a new contact lens wearer or switching from another daily brand — the 30-pack is the sensible starting point. A 30-lens box gives you a full month of daily wear to evaluate comfort, vision, and fit before committing to a larger supply. 📅 Part-Time or Occasional Wearers For wearers who use contact lenses only on certain days — weekends, sport, social occasions, or travel — the 30-pack aligns naturally with the pace of consumption. A box that lasts 1–3 months at part-time wear rates means stock stays fresh and within its expiry window without overstocking. 💊  Changes Expected If your  is due for review, or if your eye care professional has indicated a change may be needed, the 30-pack limits the amount of stock that could become the wrong power. Once your  is confirmed and stable, upgrading to the 90-pack at contactlens365.com delivers a lower per-lens cost. ✅ Same Lens as the 90 Pack The 30-pack contains the identical Biotrue ONEday lens — same Nesofilcon A HyperGel material, same 78% water content, same HD Optics, same UV protection. The only difference between the 30 and 90-pack is quantity and per-unit cost. Once you are confident in the lens, the 90-pack offers the better value. Who Is Biotrue ONEday For? Biotrue ONEday's bio-inspired design, all-day moisture retention, and daily replacement schedule make it suitable for a wide range of wearers. The following profiles benefit most from choosing Biotrue ONEday over conventional daily or monthly alternatives. 💧 Dry Eye & Comfort-Sensitive Wearers The patented dehydration barrier and 78% water content work together to resist the progressive moisture loss that causes end-of-day discomfort. Wearers who have experienced dryness and irritation with other daily lenses — particularly in air-conditioned offices or during long screen sessions — frequently report significantly better comfort with Biotrue ONEday. 💻 Screen-Heavy Professionals Reduced blink rate during prolonged screen use accelerates tear film evaporation and lens dehydration. Biotrue ONEday's dehydration barrier is specifically engineered to resist this drying effect — maintaining consistent moisture and comfort through 8-10 hour desk-based work sessions without rewetting drops. 🌞 Outdoor & Active Wearers Built-in UVA and UVB protection adds a layer of defence against solar UV radiation for outdoor wearers. Combined with the daily replacement schedule — which eliminates the deposit and contamination risks of extended lens wear — Biotrue ONEday is a strong daily choice for active, outdoor lifestyles. 🌙 Night Drivers & Low-Light Users The aspheric HD Optics design reduces halos and glare caused by spherical aberration — providing notably cleaner, sharper vision in low-light conditions compared to standard spherical daily lenses. For wearers who drive at night or frequently work in variable lighting, this is a tangible optical advantage. 🧼 Hygiene-First Wearers Daily replacement eliminates the cleaning, storage, and case-maintenance routine entirely. Each morning begins with a sterile lens from a sealed blister pack — no accumulated deposits, no solution chemistry concerns, no case contamination risk. The highest achievable standard of lens hygiene. ✈️ Travellers & Occasional Wearers The daily format is ideal for travel — no lens case, no multipurpose solution, no risk of solution spillage in luggage. Lenses are discarded at the end of each day. The 30-pack's compact size makes it easy to carry in hand luggage, and ordering a fresh box as needed avoids carrying excess stock when travelling light. The Biotrue® ONEday Lens Family Biotrue ONEday is the spherical single-vision lens in Bausch + Lomb's Biotrue daily range — all variants share the same Nesofilcon A HyperGel platform with 78% water content and Surface Active Technology. The complete family is available at contactlens365.com. This Product Biotrue® ONEday Daily spherical lens. HyperGel 78% water content, HD Optics, UV protection. Myopia and hyperopia. Also Available Biotrue® ONEday for Presbyopia Daily multifocal lens for presbyopia. 3-Zone Progressive Design for near, intermediate, and distance vision. ADD Low & High. Daily Wear Guide — Biotrue ONEday As a daily disposable lens, Biotrue ONEday requires no cleaning or storage routine — the lens is worn for one day and discarded. The following steps ensure safe and comfortable daily wear. 1Wash and dry your hands before handling any lens. Use soap and water, then dry with a clean lint-free towel. This step is just as important with daily lenses as with any other lens type — unwashed hands introduce bacteria and debris directly to the lens surface. 2Open the blister carefully. Peel back the foil seal and tip the lens and saline solution gently into the palm of your hand or directly onto your fingertip. The light blue handling tint makes the lens easy to see. Inspect the lens for any tears or defects before proceeding. 3Check orientation before insertion. The lens should form a smooth, even bowl shape with edges curving inward. If the edges flare outward, the lens is inside out — gently flip it before inserting. Biotrue ONEday does not have an inside-out indicator mark, so the bowl check is essential. 4Insert gently and blink to centre. Hold your lids open, place the lens on the cornea, and blink normally. The lens should centre quickly on the eye. If it feels uncomfortable immediately, remove it and check for debris or incorrect orientation. 5Wear for one day only. Biotrue ONEday is a single-use lens — never reuse, rehydrate, or store a used lens overnight. The lens is designed to be worn for one day and discarded at the end of the day, regardless of how comfortable it feels. 6Remove before sleep and water exposure. Always remove Biotrue ONEday lenses before sleeping, napping, swimming, showering, or entering any body of water. Daily wear lenses are not  for overnight use, and water exposure carries a significant risk of serious eye infection. 7Discard after removal. Once removed, the lens is finished — discard it immediately. Do not attempt to rinse and reinsert, even for a short period. Each blister contains exactly one lens for exactly one day's wear. ☀️ UV protection reminder: Biotrue ONEday provides UVA and UVB blocking as part of the lens design — but contact lenses do not cover the full eye and surrounding area. UV-blocking contact lenses should always be used alongside, not instead of, UV-protective sunglasses for outdoor activities or prolonged sun exposure. If anything is not right with your order, we make it simple and fast to resolve. Frequently Asked Questions — Biotrue ONEday 30 Pack What is HyperGel and how is it different from regular hydrogel? HyperGel is Bausch + Lomb's proprietary bio-inspired material (Nesofilcon A) used exclusively in the Biotrue ONEday range. Unlike conventional hydrogels, HyperGel incorporates Surface Active Technology — a process that orients the hydrophilic part of the polymer towards the lens surface to mimic the tear film's lipid layer. This creates a surface that resists dehydration more effectively than standard hydrogel coatings. Combined with the patented dehydration barrier built into the polymer matrix, HyperGel retains approximately 98% of its moisture for up to 16 hours — performance that conventional hydrogels cannot match. How long does one Biotrue ONEday 30 Pack last? One 30-lens box provides a 1-month supply for one eye, based on daily wear (one lens per day, every day). For both eyes worn daily, two boxes — one per eye — cover a full month. If you wear lenses part-time, one box may last considerably longer. Regular full-time wearers of both eyes typically find the 90-pack at contactlens365.com a more economical choice per lens. Can I reuse Biotrue ONEday lenses or wear them for more than one day? No. Biotrue ONEday is a single-use daily disposable lens — each lens is designed, tested, and  for exactly one day of wear only. Reusing the lens beyond a single day introduces the risk of deposit accumulation, microbial contamination, and ocular infection, regardless of how comfortable the lens feels. Remove and discard after each day's wear without exception. Does Biotrue ONEday really retain 78% water content all day? Biotrue ONEday starts with a 78% water content — matched to the cornea — and its patented dehydration barrier is clinically tested to retain approximately 98% of that moisture for up to 16 hours of wear. The dehydration barrier is built into the Nesofilcon A polymer matrix itself, not added as a surface coating that depletes. This is the mechanism behind the all-day comfort that distinguishes Biotrue ONEday from standard high-water-content hydrogels that begin to dehydrate within hours of insertion. Does Biotrue ONEday have UV protection? Yes. Biotrue ONEday provides UVA and UVB blocking protection as part of the lens design. However, contact lenses do not cover the entire eye and surrounding area, so UV-blocking sunglasses should always be worn alongside contact lenses for full UV protection during outdoor activities. The UV protection in the lens is an additional layer of defence, not a replacement for protective eyewear. Can Biotrue ONEday correct astigmatism or presbyopia? The standard Biotrue ONEday spherical lens does not correct astigmatism or presbyopia. Bausch + Lomb offers Biotrue ONEday for Astigmatism (toric daily, CYL –0.75 to –2.25 D) and Biotrue ONEday for Presbyopia (multifocal daily, ADD Low and High) — both using the same HyperGel platform — for these needs. All variants are available at contactlens365.com. Is Biotrue ONEday suitable for dry eye sufferers? Biotrue ONEday is one of the most frequently recommended daily lenses for wearers with mild to moderate contact lens-related dryness, specifically because of its patented dehydration barrier and 78% water content. Many wearers who experienced end-of-day dryness with other daily lenses report improved comfort with Biotrue ONEday. If you have clinically diagnosed dry eye, consult your eye care professional before choosing a lens type — individual suitability varies and professional assessment is essential. What is the difference between the Biotrue ONEday 30 Pack and 90 Pack? The lens itself is identical in both packs — same Nesofilcon A HyperGel material, same 78% water content, same HD Optics, same UV protection. The 90-pack contains 90 lenses and delivers a lower per-lens cost, making it the more economical choice for regular full-time daily wearers. The 30-pack is the better choice for first-time users, part-time wearers, or those expecting a  change. Once you have confirmed Biotrue ONEday suits your eyes, switching to the 90-pack at contactlens365.com maximises value. What is the current price for Biotrue ONEday 30 Pack? For current pricing and any active promotions, visit the Biotrue ONEday 30 Pack product page at contactlens365.com. } .tech-row { flex-direction: column; } } Bausch + Lomb · Biotrue contactlens365.com Biotrue® ONEday90 Pack Bausch + Lomb's bio-inspired daily disposable contact lens — HyperGel™ material with 78% water content matching the cornea's own biology, patented dehydration barrier retaining moisture for up to 16 hours, High Definition Optics, and built-in UVA/UVB protection. 90 lenses per box — a full 3-month supply for one eye. ✦ Daily Disposable 90 Lenses / Box HyperGel™ · 78% H₂O HD Optics · Aspheric UVA/UVB Protection BC 8.6 mm · Dia 14.2 mm DailyDisposable 78%Water Content 16hMoisture Retention 90Lenses / Box ✓ 100% Genuine 📅 Expiry Date Checked 🚚 Fast Shipping 🔒 Secure Checkout ↩ Easy Returns Biotrue ONEday 90 Pack — Daily Disposable Contact Lens The Bausch + Lomb Biotrue® ONEday 90 Pack is a daily disposable contact lens built around a single design philosophy: working with the eye's own biology rather than against it. Where most contact lenses are engineered as dry polymer materials and then treated to improve wettability, Biotrue ONEday starts from the opposite direction — its HyperGel™ material is bio-inspired from the ground up, with a 78% water content that precisely matches the natural water content of the human cornea, and a surface architecture that mimics the lipid layer of the tear film. The result is a daily lens that feels remarkably natural on the eye — and stays that way for up to 16 hours. Technical Specifications Parameter Value Brand Name Biotrue® ONEday Manufacturer Bausch + Lomb Material Nesofilcon A (HyperGel™) Material Technology Surface Active Technology™ · Patented Dehydration Barrier Water Content 78% Oxygen Transmissibility (Dk/t) 42 (at –3.00 D) Lens Design Aspheric — High Definition™ Optics Base Curve (BC) 8.6 mm Diameter (DIA) 14.2 mm UV Protection Yes — UVA & UVB blocking Handling Tint Yes (light blue visibility tint) Power Range (SPH) –12.00 to +6.00 D (0.25 D steps) Astigmatism Correction No (spherical; see Biotrue ONEday for Astigmatism) Modality Daily disposable — single use, discard after each day Extended / Overnight Wear Not  — remove before sleep Pack Contents 90 Lenses Key Technology: HyperGel™, Dehydration Barrier & HD Optics HyperGel™ · Nesofilcon A HyperGel is Bausch + Lomb's bio-inspired lens material — a unique hydrogel formulation that achieves 78% water content, precisely matching the natural water content of the human cornea. Unlike conventional hydrogels or silicone hydrogels, HyperGel incorporates Surface Active Technology™, which orients the hydrophilic component of the material towards the lens surface. This creates an inherently wettable outer surface that mimics the lipid layer of the natural tear film — reducing dehydration and maintaining the lens's moist, comfortable character throughout the wearing day without relying on added surface coatings that deplete over time. Patented Dehydration Barrier The most common cause of daily lens discomfort is progressive dehydration — as the wearing day extends, lenses lose water to the environment and to the tear film, becoming stiffer and less comfortable. Biotrue ONEday's patented dehydration barrier is built into the Nesofilcon A polymer structure itself, actively resisting water loss from the lens matrix. In Bausch + Lomb studies, this results in moisture retention of approximately 98% after 16 hours of wear — a figure that far exceeds most competing daily disposable lenses and explains why Biotrue ONEday wearers consistently report all-day comfort even in dry or air-conditioned environments. High Definition™ Optics · Aspheric Biotrue ONEday incorporates an aspheric optical design that corrects spherical aberration — the distortion introduced by standard spherical lens designs that causes halos, glare, and reduced contrast sensitivity. The aspheric profile progressively flattens from the optical centre to the lens periphery, maintaining sharp focus and high contrast across the full optical zone. The result is notably crisp, clear vision in all lighting conditions — including low light situations where spherical aberration is most visually disruptive. The combination of HyperGel's cornea-matched water content, the patented dehydration barrier, and the aspheric HD Optics design makes Biotrue ONEday one of the most comprehensively engineered daily disposables in Bausch + Lomb's range. It delivers comfort and vision performance typically associated with premium lenses, within the hygiene and convenience of a daily replacement schedule. Why Choose the 90 Pack? Biotrue ONEday is available in 30-lens and 90-lens boxes. The only difference is quantity and per-unit cost. Who Is Biotrue ONEday For? Biotrue ONEday's bio-inspired design, all-day moisture retention, and daily replacement schedule make it suitable for a wide range of wearers. The following profiles benefit most from choosing Biotrue ONEday over conventional daily or monthly alternatives. 💧 Dry Eye & Comfort-Sensitive Wearers The patented dehydration barrier and 78% water content work together to resist the progressive moisture loss that causes end-of-day discomfort. Wearers who have experienced dryness and irritation with other daily lenses — particularly in air-conditioned offices or during long screen sessions — frequently report significantly better comfort with Biotrue ONEday. 💻 Screen-Heavy Professionals Reduced blink rate during prolonged screen use accelerates tear film evaporation and lens dehydration. Biotrue ONEday's dehydration barrier is specifically engineered to resist this drying effect — maintaining consistent moisture and comfort through 8-10 hour desk-based work sessions without rewetting drops. 🌞 Outdoor & Active Wearers Built-in UVA and UVB protection adds a layer of defence against solar UV radiation for outdoor wearers. Combined with the daily replacement schedule — which eliminates the deposit and contamination risks of extended lens wear — Biotrue ONEday is a strong daily choice for active, outdoor lifestyles. 🌙 Night Drivers & Low-Light Users The aspheric HD Optics design reduces halos and glare caused by spherical aberration — providing notably cleaner, sharper vision in low-light conditions compared to standard spherical daily lenses. For wearers who drive at night or frequently work in variable lighting, this is a tangible optical advantage. 🧼 Hygiene-First Wearers Daily replacement eliminates the cleaning, storage, and case-maintenance routine entirely. Each morning begins with a sterile lens from a sealed blister pack — no accumulated deposits, no solution chemistry concerns, no case contamination risk. The highest achievable standard of lens hygiene. ✈️ Travellers & Occasional Wearers The daily format is ideal for travel — no lens case, no multipurpose solution, no risk of solution spillage in luggage. Lenses are discarded at the end of each day. The 90-pack's compact size also means three months of supply in a single box, minimising packing space. Daily Wear Guide — Biotrue ONEday As a daily disposable lens, Biotrue ONEday requires no cleaning or storage routine — the lens is worn for one day and discarded. The following steps ensure safe and comfortable daily wear. 1Wash and dry your hands before handling any lens. Use soap and water, then dry with a clean lint-free towel. This step is just as important with daily lenses as with any other lens type — unwashed hands introduce bacteria and debris directly to the lens surface. 2Open the blister carefully. Peel back the foil seal and tip the lens and saline solution gently into the palm of your hand or directly onto your fingertip. The light blue handling tint makes the lens easy to see. Inspect the lens for any tears or defects before proceeding. 3Check orientation before insertion. The lens should form a smooth, even bowl shape with edges curving inward. If the edges flare outward, the lens is inside out — gently flip it before inserting. Biotrue ONEday does not have an inside-out indicator mark, so the bowl check is essential. 4Insert gently and blink to centre. Hold your lids open, place the lens on the cornea, and blink normally. The lens should centre quickly on the eye. If it feels uncomfortable immediately, remove it and check for debris or incorrect orientation. 5Wear for one day only. Biotrue ONEday is a single-use lens — never reuse, rehydrate, or store a used lens overnight. The lens is designed to be worn for one day and discarded at the end of the day, regardless of how comfortable it feels. 6Remove before sleep and water exposure. Always remove Biotrue ONEday lenses before sleeping, napping, swimming, showering, or entering any body of water. Daily wear lenses are not  for overnight use, and water exposure carries a significant risk of serious eye infection. 7Discard after removal. Once removed, the lens is finished — discard it immediately. Do not attempt to rinse and reinsert, even for a short period. Each blister contains exactly one lens for exactly one day's wear. ☀️ UV protection reminder: Biotrue ONEday provides UVA and UVB blocking as part of the lens design — but contact lenses do not cover the full eye and surrounding area. UV-blocking contact lenses should always be used alongside, not instead of, UV-protective sunglasses for outdoor activities or prolonged sun exposure. If anything is not right with your order, we make it simple and fast to resolve. Frequently Asked Questions — Biotrue ONEday 90 Pack What is HyperGel and how is it different from regular hydrogel? HyperGel is Bausch + Lomb's proprietary bio-inspired material (Nesofilcon A) used exclusively in the Biotrue ONEday range. Unlike conventional hydrogels, HyperGel incorporates Surface Active Technology — a process that orients the hydrophilic part of the polymer towards the lens surface to mimic the tear film's lipid layer. This creates a surface that resists dehydration more effectively than standard hydrogel coatings. Combined with the patented dehydration barrier built into the polymer matrix, HyperGel retains approximately 98% of its moisture for up to 16 hours — performance that conventional hydrogels cannot match. How long does one Biotrue ONEday 90 Pack last? One 90-lens box provides a 3-month supply for one eye, based on daily wear (one lens per day, every day). For both eyes, two boxes provide a 45-day supply — or one box per eye gives a full 3-month supply for binocular daily wear. Two 90-packs — one per eye — is the standard 3-month order for both eyes. Can I reuse Biotrue ONEday lenses or wear them for more than one day? No. Biotrue ONEday is a single-use daily disposable lens — each lens is designed, tested, and  for exactly one day of wear only. Reusing the lens beyond a single day introduces the risk of deposit accumulation, microbial contamination, and ocular infection, regardless of how comfortable the lens feels. Remove and discard after each day's wear without exception. Does Biotrue ONEday really retain 78% water content all day? Biotrue ONEday starts with a 78% water content — matched to the cornea — and its patented dehydration barrier is clinically tested to retain approximately 98% of that moisture for up to 16 hours of wear. The dehydration barrier is built into the Nesofilcon A polymer matrix itself, not added as a surface coating that depletes. This is the mechanism behind the all-day comfort that distinguishes Biotrue ONEday from standard high-water-content hydrogels that begin to dehydrate within hours of insertion. Does Biotrue ONEday have UV protection? Yes. Biotrue ONEday provides UVA and UVB blocking protection as part of the lens design. However, contact lenses do not cover the entire eye and surrounding area, so UV-blocking sunglasses should always be worn alongside contact lenses for full UV protection during outdoor activities. The UV protection in the lens is an additional layer of defence, not a replacement for protective eyewear. Can Biotrue ONEday correct astigmatism or presbyopia? The standard Biotrue ONEday spherical lens does not correct astigmatism or presbyopia. Bausch + Lomb offers Biotrue ONEday for Astigmatism (toric daily, CYL –0.75 to –2.25 D) and Biotrue ONEday for Presbyopia (multifocal daily, ADD Low and High) — both using the same HyperGel platform — for these needs. All variants are available at contactlens365.com. Is Biotrue ONEday suitable for dry eye sufferers? Biotrue ONEday is one of the most frequently recommended daily lenses for wearers with mild to moderate contact lens-related dryness, specifically because of its patented dehydration barrier and 78% water content. Many wearers who experienced end-of-day dryness with other daily lenses report improved comfort with Biotrue ONEday. If you have clinically diagnosed dry eye, consult your eye care professional before choosing a lens type — individual suitability varies and professional assessment is essential. What is the difference between the 30-pack and 90-pack Biotrue ONEday? The lens itself is identical — same Nesofilcon A material, same HyperGel properties, same HD Optics, same UV protection. The 90-pack contains 90 lenses instead of 30, providing three times the supply per box. The per-lens cost is lower in the 90-pack, making it the more economical choice for regular daily wearers. The 30-pack is better suited to trial use or occasional wear. What is the current price for Biotrue ONEday 90 Pack? For current pricing and any active promotions, visit the Biotrue ONEday 90 Pack product page at contactlens365.com. Wear ✓ 100% Genuine 📅 Expiry Date Checked 🚚 Fast Shipping 🔒 Secure Checkout ↩ Easy Returns PureVision 2 HD 6 Pack — Monthly Silicone Hydrogel Contact Lens The Bausch + Lomb PureVision® 2 HD 6 Pack is one of the most oxygen-permeable monthly contact lenses available — a second-generation silicone hydrogel lens built on the Balafilcon A platform that set the benchmark for high-oxygen monthly wear. With a Dk/t of 130, aspheric High Definition Optics that reduce halos and glare, and approval for extended wear of up to 30 consecutive nights, it delivers a level of performance rarely matched in the monthly replacement category. Order PureVision 2 HD online at contactlens365.com with 100%  authenticity,  expiry dates, and fast same-day dispatch across all available powers. The "HD" designation is not marketing language — it refers to the lens's aspheric optical design, which actively corrects spherical aberration: the optical distortion introduced by standard spherical contact lenses that causes halos, glare, and reduced contrast sensitivity, particularly in low-light conditions such as night driving. Standard spherical lenses focus light to a single point on the optical axis but introduce progressive blur towards the periphery of the lens. The PureVision 2 HD's aspheric profile progressively flattens from centre to edge, maintaining focus quality across the full optical zone. The result is sharper, higher-contrast vision in precisely the conditions where ordinary lenses fall short. 🔬 High Definition Optics: The aspheric design of PureVision 2 HD corrects spherical aberration — reducing halos, glare, and blur in low-light conditions that standard spherical lenses cannot address. Clearer vision at night, in variable lighting, and at the edges of your visual field. Built on Balafilcon A with plasma surface , the PureVision 2 HD achieves a Dk/t of 130 — delivering corneal oxygen levels that comfortably exceed the clinical threshold for healthy long-term lens wear. At just 0.07 mm centre thickness (at –3.00 D), it is one of the thinnest monthly lenses available, while ComfortMoist™ packaging technology pre-hydrates the lens with a moisturising agent before it ever reaches your eye. All parameters from –12.00 to +6.00 D are stocked at contactlens365.com for prompt dispatch. Technical Specifications Parameter Value Brand Name PureVision® 2 HD (also: PureVision 2) Manufacturer Bausch + Lomb Material Balafilcon A (Silicone Hydrogel, plasma treated) Water Content 36% Oxygen Transmissibility (Dk/t) 130 (at –3.00 D) Lens Design Aspheric — High Definition™ Optics Base Curve (BC) 8.6 mm Diameter (DIA) 14.0 mm Centre Thickness 0.07 mm (at –3.00 D) UV Protection None Handling Tint Yes (light visibility tint) Power Range (SPH) –12.00 to +6.00 D (0.25 D steps) Astigmatism Correction No (spherical only) Replacement Schedule Monthly (30 days) Extended Wear  up to 30 consecutive nights (ECP guidance required) Pack Contents 6 Lenses Key Technology: Balafilcon A, HD Optics & ComfortMoist™ Balafilcon A · Dk/t 130 Balafilcon A is a plasma-treated silicone hydrogel with 36% water content and a Dk/t of 130 — one of the highest oxygen transmissibility values in any monthly contact lens. The plasma surface  converts the lens surface to a highly wettable, hydrophilic state, overcoming the natural hydrophobicity of silicone. This produces a smooth, deposit-resistant surface that maintains consistent comfort throughout the 30-day replacement cycle without relying on added wetting agents that deplete over time. High Definition™ Optics The aspheric optical design of PureVision 2 HD corrects spherical aberration — the optical imperfection that causes standard spherical lenses to produce halos around lights, reduce contrast sensitivity, and blur peripheral vision, especially in low-light conditions. By progressively flattening the lens curvature from centre to edge, the HD Optics profile maintains sharp focus across the entire optical zone. The clinical impact is most noticeable during night driving and in dimly lit environments where pupil dilation increases exposure to peripheral lens optics. ComfortMoist™ Technology PureVision 2 HD lenses are packaged using ComfortMoist™ technology — a blister pack formulation that incorporates poloaximine, a moisturising surfactant. The lens is pre-conditioned in this solution before it reaches your eye, resulting in improved comfort from the moment of first insertion. This is particularly beneficial for new wearers and those with sensitivity to the initial feel of a fresh monthly lens straight from the blister pack. Together, Balafilcon A's plasma-treated surface, HD Optics' aberration control, and ComfortMoist™ packaging address three distinct dimensions of monthly lens performance: corneal oxygen supply, optical quality in all conditions, and first-insertion comfort. These properties make the PureVision 2 HD a particularly strong choice for high-demand wearers — those with longer wearing hours, challenging visual environments, or a history of lens-related dryness or visual disturbance at night. Who Is the PureVision 2 HD For? The PureVision 2 HD monthly lens is designed for wearers who demand more from their contact lens than basic refractive correction. Its high oxygen performance, aspheric optics, extended wear capability, and wide power range make it suitable for a broad range of demanding wear profiles. 🌙 Night Drivers & Low-Light Users The aspheric HD Optics design directly targets the halos and glare that standard spherical lenses produce under low light — a critical advantage for anyone who drives at night, works in variable lighting, or notices visual disturbance after dark in standard monthly lenses. 💨 High Oxygen-Demand Wearers At Dk/t 130, PureVision 2 HD delivers corneal oxygenation well above the levels achievable with standard hydrogel lenses. Wearers with longer daily wearing hours, those who have been advised to prioritise high-oxygen lenses, and those transitioning from hydrogel to silicone hydrogel all benefit from this oxygen performance. 🌙 Extended Wear Candidates  for up to 30 consecutive nights of wear (subject to individual eye care professional assessment), PureVision 2 HD is one of very few monthly lenses with genuine clinical approval for overnight use. Extended wear suitability requires  ECP evaluation — not all wearers qualify regardless of Dk/t. 💻 Long-Day & Screen-Heavy Wearers The combination of Balafilcon A's deposit-resistant plasma surface and ComfortMoist™ pre-packaging means PureVision 2 HD is engineered to perform consistently from first insertion to end of a demanding 14-16 hour wearing day — without the progressive comfort degradation seen in lower-specification monthly lenses. 📋 High Myopia — Up to –12.00 D With a power range extending to –12.00 D, PureVision 2 HD covers prescriptions that many competing monthly lenses cannot accommodate. The high-Dk/t Balafilcon A material is especially important for high myopes, whose thicker lens centres create a greater barrier to oxygen transmission in lower-Dk materials. 🔬 Visual Quality-Conscious Wearers Wearers who have previously noticed halos, starburst effects, or reduced contrast with standard spherical monthly lenses — and who have been told they have significant levels of spherical aberration — are the precise target for HD Optics aspheric design correction. PureVision 2 HD vs the Original PureVision — What Changed? Both lenses share the Balafilcon A silicone hydrogel platform and the same fundamental oxygen performance at Dk/t 130 — but the PureVision 2 HD represents a meaningful optical and comfort upgrade over the original PureVision in three areas. First, the original PureVision used a spherical optical design; PureVision 2 HD upgraded to a full aspheric HD Optics profile for aberration correction and improved low-light clarity. Second, ComfortMoist™ blister pack technology replaced standard saline packaging, improving first-insertion comfort from day one. Third, the centre thickness was reduced — making PureVision 2 HD one of the thinnest monthly silicone hydrogel lenses available, which benefits handling and on-eye sensation particularly at lower powers. ℹ️ Note on packaging: Some PureVision 2 HD boxes in circulation no longer display the "HD" logo following a packaging update by Bausch + Lomb. The lenses inside are unchanged — the same Balafilcon A material, same aspheric HD Optics, same specifications. If your box reads PureVision 2 without the HD designation, the lens is identical. The PureVision® 2 Lens Family PureVision 2 HD is the spherical single-vision lens in Bausch + Lomb's PureVision 2 range — all built on the same Balafilcon A high-oxygen platform. How to Wear & Care for PureVision 2 HD Lenses The steps below cover standard daily wear. For wearers  for extended wear, your eye care professional's specific guidance takes precedence and will include additional monitoring requirements. 1Wash and dry your hands before every lens handling session. Use soap and water, then dry thoroughly with a lint-free towel. Moisture and fibres from hands are a common source of lens contamination. 2Inspect before insertion. Check for any tears, discolouration, or surface defects. The handling tint makes the lens easy to see on your fingertip. Confirm the lens forms a smooth, even bowl — not inside out — before placing it on your eye. 3Insert and blink to settle. Place the lens on the cornea with lids held open. Blink normally to centre the lens. PureVision 2 HD's aspheric design means optimal optical performance is centred — a well-centred lens is important for the HD Optics to function correctly. 4Rub and rinse after every removal. Rub both surfaces with multipurpose solution for at least 5 seconds per side, then rinse thoroughly. Always rub — regardless of solution labelling. Mechanical rubbing removes surface deposits that rinsing alone cannot dislodge. 5Store in fresh solution only. Empty the lens case completely and refill with fresh multipurpose solution every time. Never top up used solution — doing so dilutes the disinfecting concentration and raises infection risk. 6Maintain your lens case daily. Rinse with multipurpose solution (never tap water), wipe with a tissue, and leave face-down to air dry. Replace the case every 1–3 months. 7Replace every 30 days. Note the opening date on each lens. Even with Balafilcon A's plasma-treated deposit-resistant surface, no monthly lens should be worn beyond its replacement schedule. 8For extended wear: Sleep in PureVision 2 HD only if your eye care professional has specifically assessed and  extended wear for your individual eyes. High Dk/t alone does not make overnight wear safe for everyone — individual corneal physiology and ocular health determine suitability. ECP- extended wearers should attend more frequent professional monitoring appointments. 9Remove before water exposure. Always remove lenses before swimming, showering, or using a hot tub. All water sources — tap, pool, sea — carry pathogens that can cause serious infections on a contact lens surface. 💡 HD Optics tip: The aspheric correction in PureVision 2 HD is centred on the optical axis of the lens — for best visual results, ensure the lens is well centred on your cornea after insertion. If you notice vision quality is better on some days than others, this may relate to lens centration or fit. Consult your eye care professional if the issue is consistent. If anything is not right with your order, we make it simple to resolve. Frequently Asked Questions — PureVision 2 HD 6 Pack What does "HD" mean in PureVision 2 HD? HD stands for High Definition — a reference to the lens's aspheric optical design. Standard spherical contact lenses introduce spherical aberration, which causes halos around lights and reduced contrast at the edges of vision, particularly in low light. PureVision 2 HD's aspheric profile corrects this aberration across the optical zone, delivering sharper, higher-contrast vision — especially noticeable during night driving and in dim environments. It is an optical specification, not a marketing term. Is PureVision 2 HD the same as PureVision 2? Yes. Following a packaging update by Bausch + Lomb, some boxes of PureVision 2 HD no longer carry the "HD" designation and are labelled simply as PureVision 2. The lens inside is identical — same Balafilcon A material, same aspheric HD Optics, same specifications, same extended wear approval. If your box reads PureVision 2, the product is unchanged. How long does one PureVision 2 HD 6 Pack last? Each lens is replaced monthly, so one 6-lens box provides a 6-month supply for one eye. For both eyes, two boxes — one per eye — give a full 3-month binocular supply. Two boxes per order is the most practical purchase for full coverage. Can I sleep in PureVision 2 HD lenses? PureVision 2 HD is  for extended wear of up to 30 consecutive nights — but this applies only when an eye care professional has individually assessed your suitability and given  approval. A high Dk/t value does not make overnight wear universally safe; individual corneal physiology, ocular health, and lifestyle factors all affect suitability. Do not sleep in PureVision 2 HD without ECP-specific approval and regular professional monitoring. Why does PureVision 2 HD have only 36% water content? In silicone hydrogel lenses, oxygen transmissibility comes primarily from the silicone component of the material — not the water content. Balafilcon A achieves its Dk/t of 130 through its silicone matrix, not through high water levels. The plasma surface  then resolves the wettability issue that untreated silicone creates, giving a comfortable, hydrophilic lens surface despite the lower water percentage. For silicone hydrogels, water content and oxygen performance are largely independent variables. Can PureVision 2 HD correct astigmatism? No. PureVision 2 HD is a spherical lens — it corrects myopia and hyperopia only. If your  includes a cylindrical component, you need a toric lens. PureVision 2 Toric is available at contactlens365.com for astigmatism correction on the same Balafilcon A platform. Is PureVision 2 HD good for dry eyes? PureVision 2 HD's Balafilcon A plasma surface  creates a wettable, deposit-resistant lens surface that maintains consistent comfort across the wearing day. Its 36% water content means the lens retains more of its own water than high-water hydrogels, which can dehydrate on the eye in dry environments. Many wearers who experienced dryness with hydrogel lenses report improved comfort after switching to PureVision 2 HD. Individual suitability for dry eye varies — consult your eye care professional for personalised advice. Does PureVision 2 HD have UV protection? No. PureVision 2 HD does not include UV-blocking capability. No contact lens provides sufficient protection to replace UV-protective eyewear for outdoor use. If UV protection is important for your lifestyle, discuss lens options with your eye care professional. What is the current price for PureVision 2 HD 6 Pack? For current pricing and any active promotions, visit the PureVision 2 HD 6 Pack product page at contactlens365.com.
