Also known as PureVision 2 for Presbyopia. 6 lenses per box. ✦ Monthly Multifocal For Presbyopia 6 Lenses / Box 3-Zone Progressive Design Balafilcon A · Dk/t 130 ADD: Low & High MonthlyReplacement 130Dk/t O₂ Transmit. 36%Water Content 3-ZoneProgressive Design ✓ 100% Genuine 📅 Expiry Date Checked 🚚 Fast Shipping 🔒 Secure Checkout ↩ Easy Returns PureVision 2 Multifocal 6 Pack — Monthly Contact Lens for Presbyopia The Bausch + Lomb PureVision® 2 Multifocal 6 Pack — also referred to as PureVision 2 for Presbyopia — is Bausch + Lomb's flagship monthly multifocal contact lens, designed specifically for wearers whose near vision has been affected by presbyopia. It delivers clear, comfortable vision at near, intermediate, and distance in a single monthly silicone hydrogel lens — without reading glasses, without bifocals. Order PureVision 2 Multifocal online at contactlens365.com with 100%  authenticity,  expiry dates, and fast same-day dispatch. Presbyopia is the age-related loss of the eye's ability to focus on close objects — it typically begins in the early-to-mid forties and progresses gradually. For existing contact lens wearers, presbyopia presents a specific challenge: how to continue wearing lenses while accommodating the need for near correction. The PureVision 2 Multifocal solves this through its proprietary 3-Zone Progressive Design — a concentric lens architecture that simultaneously presents distance, intermediate, and near correction zones to the visual system, allowing the brain to select the appropriate focus for each viewing distance in real time. The result is a single lens that handles phone screens, computer monitors, and road signs with equal fluency. 👁️ 3-Zone Progressive Design: Near, intermediate, and distance correction zones are built into a single lens. In a Bausch + Lomb study, nearly 90% of wearers reported clear vision at all distances immediately after first insertion. Built on the high-performance Balafilcon A silicone hydrogel material with a Dk/t of 130, the PureVision 2 Multifocal allows exceptional levels of oxygen to reach the cornea — making it one of the most breathable monthly multifocal lenses available. This oxygen performance, combined with its approval for extended wear of up to 30 consecutive nights (subject to eye care professional guidance), makes it an unusually flexible lens. Two ADD power options — Low and High — cover the full clinical range of presbyopic correction from +0.75 D to +2.50 D. Technical Specifications Parameter Value Brand Name PureVision® 2 Multifocal (also: PureVision 2 for Presbyopia) Manufacturer Bausch + Lomb Material Balafilcon A (Silicone Hydrogel) Water Content 36% Oxygen Transmissibility (Dk/t) 130 (at –3.00 D) Lens Design Multifocal — 3-Zone Progressive Design Base Curve (BC) 8.6 mm Diameter (DIA) 14.0 mm Centre Thickness 0.07 mm (at –3.00 D) UV Protection None Handling Tint Yes (light visibility tint) Power Range (SPH) –10.00 to +6.00 D (0.25 D steps) ADD Power Low (+0.75 to +1.50 D) / High (+1.75 to +2.50 D) Replacement Schedule Monthly (30 days) Extended Wear  up to 30 consecutive nights (ECP guidance required) Pack Contents 6 Lenses Key Technology: 3-Zone Progressive Design & Balafilcon A 3-Zone Progressive Design The PureVision 2 Multifocal uses three concentric correction zones within a single lens: a central distance zone, a surrounding intermediate zone, and an outer near zone. The design is based on simultaneous vision — all three zones are presented to the eye at once, and the visual cortex learns to select the appropriate focus automatically. This delivers the seamless, natural-feeling multifocal performance that distinguishes the PureVision 2 from older bifocal lens designs that hard-transition between just two powers. Balafilcon A · Dk/t 130 Balafilcon A is a plasma-treated silicone hydrogel with 36% water content and an industry-leading Dk/t of 130. This oxygen transmissibility level — among the highest available in a monthly multifocal lens — ensures the cornea receives the oxygen it needs for healthy long-term lens wear. The plasma surface  creates a highly wettable lens surface that resists deposits and maintains consistent comfort across the full 30-day replacement cycle. High Definition™ Optics PureVision 2 Multifocal incorporates Bausch + Lomb's High Definition Optics design — an aspheric optic profile that reduces the appearance of halos, glare, and blurriness in low-light conditions. For presbyopic wearers who drive at night or work in variable lighting, HD Optics delivers the clarity of a premium multifocal lens across the full range of everyday visual environments. Together, the 3-Zone Progressive Design, Balafilcon A silicone hydrogel, and High Definition Optics address the three central requirements of a premium monthly multifocal contact lens for presbyopia: seamless vision at all distances, corneal health through oxygen performance, and visual quality in all lighting conditions. These properties explain why the PureVision 2 Multifocal has remained the reference monthly multifocal lens for eye care professionals fitting presbyopic patients in contact lenses. Understanding ADD Powers: Low vs High Every PureVision 2 Multifocal  includes an ADD power — the additional near correction component required to compensate for presbyopia. The PureVision 2 Multifocal is available in two ADD options, covering the full clinical range of presbyopic correction. 🔵 ADD Low — +0.75 to +1.50 D The Low ADD option is prescribed for early to moderate presbyopia — wearers who have begun to notice difficulty with near tasks but do not yet require full near correction. It is the most common starting point for newly presbyopic lens wearers and provides near correction while maintaining strong distance clarity. 🟣 ADD High — +1.75 to +2.50 D The High ADD option addresses moderate to advanced presbyopia — wearers who require more significant near correction for reading, screen work, or fine detail tasks. It delivers stronger near vision while the 3-Zone Progressive Design maintains comfortable distance and intermediate clarity. 📋 ADD power note: ADD power is determined exclusively by your eye care professional during a contact lens fitting. Never self-select an ADD power based on your reading glasses  — multifocal contact lens fitting requires a dedicated eye examination and trial lens assessment. Always order the exact ADD power specified on your current contact lens . Who Is the PureVision 2 Multifocal For? The PureVision 2 Multifocal monthly lens is the appropriate choice for established contact lens wearers whose vision now requires near correction alongside their existing spherical . Its high oxygen performance, wide power range, and two ADD options make it suitable for the majority of presbyopic wearers. 👓 Presbyopia — All Stages Low ADD covers early presbyopia (+0.75 to +1.50 D); High ADD covers moderate to advanced presbyopia (+1.75 to +2.50 D). Together they span the full clinical range of presbyopic correction in a single lens design. 📱 Digital Life — Near & Intermediate The 3-Zone Progressive Design's intermediate correction zone specifically addresses the viewing distance of smartphone screens and computer monitors — the visual demand that many early presbyopes notice first, and that single-vision lenses cannot address. 🚗 Demanding Visual Environments HD Optics reduce halos and glare in low light — an important advantage for presbyopic wearers who drive at night or work in variable lighting conditions where lower-quality multifocal lens designs can produce distracting optical artefacts. 💨 Oxygen-Sensitive Wearers At Dk/t 130, the PureVision 2 Multifocal is among the most oxygen-permeable monthly multifocal lenses available — important for wearers whose eye care professionals have recommended a high-oxygen lens, or who wear lenses for extended hours each day. 🌙 Extended Wear Candidates  for up to 30 consecutive nights of wear (subject to individual assessment by an eye care professional), making the PureVision 2 Multifocal one of the very few monthly multifocal lenses with genuine extended wear capability. 🔄 Glasses-Free Lifestyle For wearers who relied on contact lenses before presbyopia onset and want to avoid switching to reading glasses or progressive spectacles, the PureVision 2 Multifocal provides a compelling spectacle-free alternative for full-time lens wear. The PureVision® 2 Lens Family The PureVision 2 Multifocal is part of Bausch + Lomb's PureVision 2 range — a family of high-oxygen monthly silicone hydrogel lenses sharing the Balafilcon A platform. The full range is available at contactlens365.com. This Product PureVision® 2 Multifocal Monthly multifocal for presbyopia. 3-Zone Progressive Design, Dk/t 130, ADD Low & High. Also called PureVision 2 for Presbyopia. Also Available PureVision® 2 HD Spherical monthly lens for myopia and hyperopia. Same Balafilcon A material, Dk/t 130 oxygen performance, and HD Optics in a single-vision design. Also Available PureVision® 2 Toric Monthly toric lens for astigmatism correction. Balafilcon A with Auto-Align Design for rotational stability on the eye. How to Wear & Care for PureVision 2 Multifocal Lenses Correct care and handling are essential for safe wear of any contact lens, including the PureVision 2 Multifocal. The following steps apply to daily wear use. For extended wear, your eye care professional will provide specific guidance that takes precedence over these general instructions. 1Wash and dry your hands before every lens handling session. Use soap and water, then dry with a clean lint-free towel. Do not handle lenses with wet or dirty hands. 2Inspect before insertion. Check each lens for tears, discolouration, or deformation. The handling tint makes the lens easy to see on your fingertip — confirm the lens forms a smooth, even bowl (not inside out) before proceeding. 3Insert and allow adaptation time. Place the lens on the cornea and blink normally. Multifocal lenses may require a brief neural adaptation period — the brain learns to select the appropriate focus zone. Most wearers adapt within a few days to two weeks of consistent daily wear. 4Rub and rinse after every removal. Rub both lens surfaces with multipurpose solution for at least 5 seconds per side, then rinse thoroughly. Always rub — regardless of the solution label — to remove deposits, protein, and debris. 5Store in fresh solution only. Empty the lens case completely before each refill. Never top up old solution — this dilutes the disinfecting agents and significantly increases the risk of contamination. 6Maintain your lens case daily. Rinse with multipurpose solution (never tap water), wipe with a clean tissue, and leave face-down to air dry. Replace the case every 1–3 months. 7Replace every 30 days. Note the date you open each lens. Do not extend the monthly replacement schedule regardless of how the lens feels — deposit accumulation affects both comfort and optical performance over time. 8For extended wear: Only sleep in PureVision 2 Multifocal lenses if your eye care professional has specifically assessed and  extended wear for your eyes. Even with the lens's high Dk/t 130 oxygen performance, overnight wear requires individual evaluation and more frequent professional monitoring. 9Remove before water exposure. Always remove lenses before swimming, showering, or entering a hot tub. All water sources carry micro-organisms that can cause serious eye infections on a contact lens surface. 💡 Multifocal adaptation tip: If vision is inconsistent in the first few days of wearing PureVision 2 Multifocal, this is normal — the visual system takes time to learn to use simultaneous vision multifocal optics. Wear the lenses consistently every day during the adaptation period. If significant dissatisfaction persists beyond two weeks, consult your eye care professional for a fitting review rather than discontinuing independently. Frequently Asked Questions — PureVision 2 Multifocal 6 Pack What is the difference between PureVision 2 Multifocal and PureVision 2 for Presbyopia? They are the same lens. PureVision 2 for Presbyopia is the full product name used by Bausch + Lomb — "multifocal" describes the lens design. Both names refer to the same Balafilcon A silicone hydrogel lens with 3-Zone Progressive Design. If your  reads either name, you are ordering the correct product at contactlens365.com. What does ADD Low and ADD High mean? ADD power is the additional near correction built into the multifocal lens to compensate for presbyopia. Low ADD (+0.75 to +1.50 D) is prescribed for early to moderate presbyopia. High ADD (+1.75 to +2.50 D) is prescribed for moderate to advanced presbyopia. Your eye care professional determines which ADD power is appropriate for your eyes during a fitting — always order the ADD power specified on your current contact lens . How long does one PureVision 2 Multifocal 6 Pack last? Each lens is a monthly replacement worn for 30 days. One 6-lens box provides a 6-month supply for one eye. For both eyes, two boxes — one per eye — give a full 3-month binocular supply. Can I sleep in PureVision 2 Multifocal lenses? PureVision 2 Multifocal is  for extended wear of up to 30 consecutive nights — but this approval applies only when an eye care professional has specifically assessed your suitability for overnight lens wear and given  guidance to do so. Sleeping in lenses without professional evaluation and approval carries a significantly elevated risk of infection and corneal damage. Do not assume approval from the lens's extended wear certification alone. My vision seems inconsistent after first inserting PureVision 2 Multifocal — is this normal? Yes. Multifocal contact lenses work on simultaneous vision principles — the visual cortex must adapt to interpreting multiple correction zones at once. This adaptation typically takes a few days to two weeks of consistent daily wear. Mild variability in vision quality during this period is expected and normal. If significant dissatisfaction persists beyond two weeks, consult your eye care professional for a fitting review. Can PureVision 2 Multifocal correct astigmatism as well as presbyopia? PureVision 2 Multifocal is a spherical multifocal lens — it does not correct astigmatism. If you have both presbyopia and astigmatism, discuss your options with your eye care professional. Depending on your cylinder power, monovision fitting or an alternative lens design may be recommended. Why does PureVision 2 Multifocal have only 36% water content if it is a silicone hydrogel? Silicone hydrogel lenses derive their oxygen transmissibility primarily from the silicone component of the material rather than from water content — which is why Balafilcon A achieves a Dk/t of 130 with only 36% water. Higher water content does not necessarily mean better comfort or oxygen performance in silicone hydrogel lenses. The plasma surface  on Balafilcon A creates the wettable, comfortable surface, while the silicone matrix delivers the oxygen. Does PureVision 2 Multifocal have UV protection? No. PureVision 2 Multifocal does not include UV-blocking capability. If UV protection is important for your lifestyle, discuss this with your eye care professional. Note that no contact lens provides sufficient UV protection to replace UV-protective eyewear for outdoor activities. What is the current price for PureVision 2 Multifocal 6 Pack? For current pricing and any active promotions, visit the PureVision 2 Multifocal 6 Pack product page at contactlens365.com. } .tech-row { flex-direction: column; } } Bausch + Lomb · SofLens contactlens365.com SofLens® 596 Pack Bausch + Lomb's proven monthly spherical contact lens — 59% water content Hilafilcon B material, UniFit™ technology for a single-parameter fit, and a deposit-resistant surface that keeps vision clear throughout the full 30-day replacement cycle. 6 lenses per box. ✦ Monthly Spherical 6 Lenses / Box UniFit™ Technology Hilafilcon B · 59% H₂O BC 8.6 mm · Dia 14.2 mm Handling Tint MonthlyReplacement 59%Water Content 22Dk/t O₂ Transmit. 6Lenses / Box ✓ 100% Genuine 📅 Expiry Date Checked 🚚 Fast Shipping 🔒 Secure Checkout ↩ Easy Returns SofLens 59 6 Pack — Monthly Contact Lens by Bausch + Lomb The Bausch + Lomb SofLens® 59 6 Pack is one of the most widely used monthly spherical contact lenses in the world. Its name tells you exactly what it delivers: a 59% water content hydrogel lens engineered to keep eyes comfortably hydrated throughout the wearing day, on a monthly replacement schedule. Order SofLens 59 online at contactlens365.com with 100%  authenticity,  expiry dates, and fast same-day dispatch on all available parameters. Built on Bausch + Lomb's Hilafilcon B polymer — a non-ionic, high-water-content hydrogel — the SofLens 59 combines three properties that have made it a long-standing choice for both first-time and experienced lens wearers: deposit resistance that maintains optical clarity across the full replacement cycle, UniFit™ technology that delivers a reliable fit across a wide range of eye sizes from a single base curve, and a handling tint that makes locating, inserting, and removing the lens significantly easier. These are not headline features — they are practical, everyday advantages that make the SofLens 59 a dependable monthly lens to live in. 💡 UniFit™ Technology: Bausch + Lomb's single-parameter fitting design means the SofLens 59's 8.6 mm base curve is engineered to fit the majority of eye shapes comfortably — simplifying the fitting process and reducing the need for trial sets. With a power range of –9.00 to +6.00 D, the SofLens 59 covers the full spectrum of common myopic and hyperopic prescriptions. Its 14.2 mm diameter and 8.6 mm base curve give it a well-centred, stable on-eye position for consistent vision quality throughout the day. All powers are stocked at contactlens365.com in the standard 6-lens monthly pack for prompt dispatch. Technical Specifications Parameter Value Brand Name Bausch + Lomb SofLens® 59 Manufacturer Bausch + Lomb Material Hilafilcon B (Hydrogel, non-ionic) Water Content 59% Lens Design Spherical — UniFit™ Technology Base Curve (BC) 8.6 mm Diameter (DIA) 14.2 mm Oxygen Transmissibility (Dk/t) 22 UV Protection None Handling Tint Yes (light visibility tint) Power Range (SPH) –9.00 to +6.00 D (0.25 D steps; 0.50 D steps from –6.00 D) Astigmatism Correction No (spherical only) Replacement Schedule Monthly (30 days) Pack Contents 6 Lenses Key Technology: Hilafilcon B & UniFit™ Hilafilcon B · Non-ionic Hydrogel Hilafilcon B is a non-ionic hydrogel polymer with 59% water content — high enough to deliver sustained on-eye hydration throughout the wearing day, while the non-ionic chemistry actively resists protein and lipid deposit accumulation. Deposits on a contact lens surface reduce clarity and increase end-of-day discomfort; Hilafilcon B's deposit-resistant profile helps the SofLens 59 perform cleanly and comfortably through day 30, not just day 1. UniFit™ Technology Bausch + Lomb's UniFit™ technology applies a single-base-curve design philosophy — the 8.6 mm base curve is engineered to provide a stable, comfortable fit across the majority of corneal shapes without the need for multiple fitting options. The result is a lens that sits centred and secure on the eye, minimises lens movement on blink, and delivers consistent vision quality — from the first insertion of the day to the last removal of the month. Handling Tint The SofLens 59 includes a light visibility tint — a faint blue tint applied to the lens that makes it significantly easier to locate in a lens case, on a fingertip, or if dropped. The tint does not affect the colour of the eye in wear. For new wearers especially, the handling tint removes one of the most common frustrations of daily lens insertion and removal routines. The combination of Hilafilcon B's deposit resistance, UniFit™'s stable fitting geometry, and the practical handling tint defines the SofLens 59's core value: a reliable, no-compromise monthly lens that performs consistently across the entire replacement cycle, fits the majority of wearers from a single parameter, and is straightforward to handle from day one. Who Is the SofLens 59 6 Pack For? The SofLens 59 monthly lens is a versatile choice that suits a broad range of wearers. Its proven hydrogel material, single-fit simplicity, deposit resistance, and wide power range make it appropriate for daily wear in virtually any lifestyle context. The following wearer profiles benefit most. 💧 Comfort & Hydration First At 59% water content, the SofLens 59 maintains a naturally moist feel throughout the wearing day. Wearers who prioritise sustained hydration over raw oxygen transmissibility — particularly those with mild lens-related dryness — often find the Hilafilcon B material well suited to their needs. 👁️ First-Time Contact Lens Wearers The SofLens 59's UniFit™ design simplifies the fitting process for eye care professionals, while the handling tint makes lens insertion and removal far easier for new wearers. The soft, comfortable Hilafilcon B material also adapts well on the eye, supporting a smooth introduction to lens wear. 💰 Budget-Conscious Monthly Wearers The SofLens 59 delivers a genuine Bausch + Lomb monthly lens at a competitive price point — making it an accessible choice for wearers who want reliable performance without paying a premium for features their lifestyle does not require. 📋 Standard Myopia & Hyperopia SPH range –9.00 to +6.00 D covers the overwhelming majority of common short-sighted and long-sighted prescriptions. The SofLens 59 is a spherical-only lens — wearers with astigmatism should consider the SofLens Toric instead. 🔄 Consistent Monthly Wearers For wearers who wear lenses every day of the month and replace on schedule, the SofLens 59's deposit-resistant Hilafilcon B material provides consistent comfort from week one to week four — without the progressive deterioration seen in lower-quality monthly hydrogels. 🔬 Deposit-Sensitive Wearers The non-ionic nature of Hilafilcon B gives the SofLens 59 a natural resistance to protein and lipid deposits, which are the primary cause of end-of-day lens discomfort and cloudy vision. For wearers whose eyes produce more than average tear proteins, the non-ionic material is a meaningful advantage. The SofLens® Monthly Lens Family The SofLens 59 is the spherical foundation of Bausch + Lomb's SofLens monthly range. All variants share the same commitment to high-water-content comfort. Simultaneous vision design for near, intermediate, and distance vision in one lens. How to Wear & Care for SofLens 59 Lenses Consistent care habits are the foundation of safe, comfortable monthly lens wear. The steps below apply to the SofLens 59 6 Pack and all monthly contact lenses. Your eye care professional's specific guidance always takes precedence. 1Wash and dry your hands before every lens handling session. Use soap and water, then dry with a lint-free towel. Moisture or fibres on your fingers can contaminate the lens surface before insertion. 2Inspect the lens before inserting. Check for tears, discolouration, or any deformation. The lens should form a smooth, even bowl. Ensure it is not inside out — the edges should curve inward. The light handling tint makes the lens easy to see on your fingertip. 3Insert gently and blink to settle. Place the lens on the cornea, hold your lids open, and blink normally. The lens should centre quickly. If it feels uncomfortable immediately after insertion, remove and inspect for debris before reinserting. 4Rub and rinse after every removal. Rub both surfaces of the lens with multipurpose solution for at least 5 seconds per side, then rinse thoroughly. Always rub — regardless of whether the solution is labelled "no-rub". The mechanical action of rubbing removes deposits and debris that rinsing alone cannot. 5Store in fresh solution every time. Empty the lens case completely and refill with fresh multipurpose solution. Never top up old solution — topping up dilutes the disinfecting agents and significantly increases infection risk. 6Clean your lens case daily. Rinse with multipurpose solution (never tap water), wipe with a clean tissue, and leave face-down to air dry. Replace the lens case every 1–3 months — a contaminated case is one of the most common sources of lens-related eye infection. 7Replace every 30 days without exception. Mark the date you open each lens. Even with Hilafilcon B's deposit-resistant material, no monthly lens should be worn beyond its replacement schedule. Extended wear increases the risk of deposit accumulation, reduced comfort, and infection. 8Remove before sleep and water exposure. Do not sleep, shower, or swim in your lenses unless your eye doctor has explicitly  overnight or extended wear. All water sources carry micro-organisms that can cause serious eye infections when introduced to a contact lens surface. 💡 Handling tip: Use the SofLens 59's light blue handling tint to your advantage — before inserting, hold the lens up to a light source to confirm it is right-side out (a smooth bowl shape) and free from debris. The tint also makes it easy to confirm the lens is in your eye after insertion, without changing the apparent colour of your eye. Frequently Asked Questions — SofLens 59 6 Pack Why is it called SofLens 59? The name refers directly to the lens material's water content: SofLens 59 is made from Hilafilcon B hydrogel with 59% water content. Bausch + Lomb named the lens after this defining characteristic — just as the SofLens 66 Toric was named for its 66% water content. The "59" is a specification, not a model generation number. How long does one SofLens 59 6 Pack last? Each lens in the box is worn for 30 days, so one 6-lens box provides a 6-month supply for one eye. For both eyes, you need two boxes — giving a full 3-month supply for both eyes. Two boxes per order is the most practical purchase for binocular coverage. Can SofLens 59 correct astigmatism or presbyopia? No. SofLens 59 is a spherical lens designed to correct myopia (short-sightedness) and hyperopia (long-sightedness) only. It does not correct astigmatism or presbyopia. If your  includes a cylindrical (CYL) component, you need a toric lens — Bausch + Lomb's SofLens Toric is available at contactlens365.com. For presbyopia, consider the SofLens Multifocal. What is UniFit™ technology and why does it matter? UniFit™ is Bausch + Lomb's single-base-curve fitting approach. Rather than offering multiple base curves, the SofLens 59's 8.6 mm base curve is designed to fit comfortably across the majority of eye shapes. This simplifies the fitting process significantly — your eye care professional can prescribe the lens based on your spherical power without needing to trial multiple base curve options in most cases. Does SofLens 59 have a handling tint? Yes. SofLens 59 features a light blue visibility tint that makes the lens significantly easier to locate in a lens case, on a fingertip, or if dropped. The tint is cosmetically inert in wear — it does not alter the visible colour of your eye. Is SofLens 59 suitable for dry eye wearers? SofLens 59's 59% water content and non-ionic Hilafilcon B material provide a naturally moist feel and low deposit accumulation — properties that benefit many wearers with mild lens-related dryness. However, suitability for dry eye is individual. If you experience significant dryness with contact lenses, consult your eye care professional before choosing a lens type. They can recommend the most appropriate option for your specific condition and wearing habits. Does SofLens 59 have UV protection? No. SofLens 59 does not include UV-blocking capability. If UV protection is a priority, discuss this with your eye care professional — they can recommend a lens that includes UV blocking. Note that no contact lens replaces UV-protective eyewear for outdoor use. Can I sleep in SofLens 59 lenses? SofLens 59 is designed for daily wear with nightly removal. Do not sleep in these lenses unless your eye care professional has specifically assessed your eyes and  extended wear. Sleeping in lenses not  for overnight wear significantly increases the risk of eye infection and corneal damage. What is the current price for SofLens 59 6 Pack? Also known as SofLens 66 Toric. 6 lenses per box. ✦ Monthly Toric For Astigmatism 6 Lenses / Box Lo-Torque Design Alphafilcon A · 66% H₂O BC 8.5 mm · Dia 14.5 mm MonthlyReplacement 66%Water Content 5CYL Powers 18Axis Options ✓ 100% Genuine 📅 Expiry Date Checked 🚚 Fast Shipping 🔒 Secure Checkout ↩ Easy Returns SofLens Toric 6 Pack — Monthly Contact Lens for Astigmatism The Bausch + Lomb SofLens® Toric 6 Pack — also sold under its original name SofLens 66 Toric — is one of the most established monthly toric contact lenses available today. Designed specifically for wearers who require astigmatism correction, it delivers consistent cylindrical correction in a soft, high-water-content hydrogel lens that remains comfortable throughout the entire 30-day replacement cycle. Order SofLens Toric online at contactlens365.com with  authenticity,  expiry dates, and fast dispatch on all in-stock parameters. Astigmatism is one of the most common refractive errors — yet it cannot be corrected by a standard spherical lens. A toric contact lens for astigmatism adds a precisely oriented cylindrical correction to the spherical power. For that correction to work reliably throughout the day, the lens must remain in the correct rotational position on the eye. The SofLens Toric achieves this through Bausch + Lomb's patented Lo-Torque stabilisation design, which minimises the forces that cause lens rotation during blinking — keeping the cylindrical axis aligned from morning to night, across the full monthly replacement cycle. 🎯 Lo-Torque Design: Bausch + Lomb's patented stabilisation system keeps the SofLens Toric correctly oriented on the eye throughout the day — so your astigmatism correction stays precisely aligned with every blink, every hour, for 30 days. With 66% water content in the Alphafilcon A hydrogel material, the SofLens Toric delivers a naturally moist, soft feel that many wearers find more comfortable than lower-water silicone hydrogels. The lens covers a broad  range — SPH –9.00 to +6.00 D, five cylinder powers from –0.75 to –2.75 D, and 18 axis options in 10° steps across the full 180° — making it a versatile fitting choice for the vast majority of astigmatic prescriptions. All available parameters are held in stock at contactlens365.com for prompt dispatch. Technical Specifications Parameter Value Brand Name SofLens® Toric (also: SofLens 66 Toric) Manufacturer Bausch + Lomb Material Alphafilcon A (Hydrogel) Water Content 66% Lens Design Toric — Lo-Torque Stabilisation Base Curve (BC) 8.5 mm Diameter (DIA) 14.5 mm Oxygen Transmissibility (Dk/t) 22 UV Protection None Power Range (SPH) –9.00 to +6.00 D Cylinder (CYL) –0.75 / –1.25 / –1.75 / –2.25 / –2.75 D Axis 10° to 180° in 10° steps (18 options) Replacement Schedule Monthly (30 days) Pack Contents 6 Lenses Key Technology: Lo-Torque Design & Alphafilcon A Lo-Torque Stabilisation Bausch + Lomb's patented Lo-Torque design uses an engineered lens geometry that minimises the rotational forces acting on the toric lens during blinking. The lens orients to the correct cylindrical axis and recovers rapidly after any disturbance — the key requirement for consistent astigmatism correction. Three laser location marks (at 5, 6, and 7 o'clock) allow eye care professionals to verify axis orientation during fitting consultations. Alphafilcon A · 66% H₂O Alphafilcon A is a non-ionic hydrogel with 66% water content. The high water level produces a naturally soft, moist feel on the eye from day one. The non-ionic polymer chemistry resists protein and lipid deposit accumulation — helping maintain lens clarity and comfort through the final days of the 30-day cycle, not just the first week. Cast-moulded manufacturing ensures consistent parameters from lens to lens — essential for reliable toric correction. Together, Lo-Torque geometry and Alphafilcon A address the two primary challenges of monthly toric lens wear: rotational stability — keeping the cylindrical correction correctly positioned — and sustained comfort — maintaining a wearable lens surface through day 30. These two properties are why the SofLens Toric has remained a reliable fitting choice for astigmatic wearers and eye care professionals alike for many years. Who Is the SofLens Toric For? The SofLens Toric monthly lens suits a wide range of astigmatic wearers. Its broad parameter coverage, proven toric geometry, and high-water-content comfort profile make it appropriate for everyday lens wear across most lifestyle contexts. The following wearer profiles benefit most from choosing the SofLens Toric 6 Pack. 🎯 All Common Astigmatic Prescriptions SPH –9.00 to +6.00 D, five CYL powers from –0.75 to –2.75 D, and 18 axis positions in 10° steps — covering with-the-rule, against-the-rule, and oblique astigmatism across the full range of common prescriptions. 💧 Comfort-Priority Wearers 66% water content gives the SofLens Toric a naturally hydrated, soft initial feel. Wearers who find lower-water silicone hydrogels uncomfortable at insertion — or who experience end-of-day lens awareness — often report a more comfortable experience with high-water hydrogels like Alphafilcon A. 💻 Screen-Heavy Professionals Prolonged screen use reduces blink rate, accelerating tear film evaporation and lens surface drying. Alphafilcon A's high water content helps sustain on-eye hydration through long desk-based work sessions without requiring rewetting drops. 🔄 Monthly Budget-Conscious Wearers Monthly toric lenses offer a significantly lower per-day cost than daily disposable torics while still providing fresh-lens comfort every month. One 6-pack covers 6 months for one eye. Two boxes provide a full 3-month supply for both eyes. 👁️ First-Time Toric Wearers The SofLens Toric's comfortable soft hydrogel material and well-established fitting profile make it a common first- choice when an eye care professional recommends toric lenses for the first time. The soft feel aids adaptation for new wearers. ✅ Existing SofLens Wearers Already using SofLens Monthly spherical lenses and developing astigmatism? The SofLens Toric extends the same Alphafilcon A comfort platform with the cylindrical correction component your updated  now requires — a natural transition with minimal adaptation time. SofLens Toric vs SofLens 66 Toric — Are They the Same Lens? Yes — SofLens Toric and SofLens 66 Toric are the same product. The original name, SofLens 66 Toric, referenced the Alphafilcon A material's 66% water content. Bausch + Lomb later simplified the product name to SofLens Toric as part of a product line rebrand. During the transition period, both names appeared on otherwise identical packaging from the same production batches. Today the lens is sold as SofLens Toric, but many prescriptions, patient records, and retailer listings still carry the SofLens 66 Toric name. If your  reads SofLens 66 Toric, the SofLens Toric 6 Pack at contactlens365.com is the correct and direct replacement — same material, same parameters, same lens. ℹ️ SofLens 66 Toric = SofLens Toric. How to Wear & Care for SofLens Toric Lenses Safe, comfortable monthly toric lens wear depends on consistent care habits. The steps below apply to the SofLens Toric 6 Pack and all monthly contact lenses. Always prioritise the specific guidance of your eye care professional. 1Wash and dry your hands with soap and water before every lens handling session. Dry with a lint-free towel — moisture and fibres on fingers can contaminate the lens surface. 2Inspect before insertion. Check for any tears, discolouration, or deformation. Confirm the lens is the correct side out — it should form a smooth, even bowl shape. A flared or uneven edge means it is inside out. 3Insert and allow to settle. Place the lens on the cornea and blink normally. Toric lenses require a few blinks to orient to the correct cylindrical axis. Allow a brief settling period before evaluating vision clarity — initial mild blur is normal. 4Rub and rinse after every removal. Rub both lens surfaces with multipurpose solution for at least 5 seconds per side, then rinse thoroughly. Always rub — even if the solution is labelled "no-rub". Rubbing removes debris and protein that rinsing alone cannot. 5Store in fresh solution only. Empty the lens case fully and refill with fresh multipurpose solution each time. Never top up used solution — diluting disinfecting agents raises infection risk. 6Maintain your lens case daily. Rinse with multipurpose solution (never tap water), wipe with a clean tissue, and air-dry face-down. Replace the case every 1–3 months. 7Replace every 30 days. Note the date you open each lens. Deposit accumulation on older lenses affects both comfort and optical performance — do not extend the monthly replacement schedule regardless of how the lens feels. 8Remove before sleep and water exposure. Do not sleep, swim, or shower in your lenses unless your eye care professional has explicitly  extended wear. All water sources — tap, pool, or natural — carry micro-organisms that can cause serious eye infections on contact lens surfaces. 💡 Toric lens tip: If you experience persistent blurred or fluctuating vision during wear, this may signal lens rotation — where the cylindrical axis has shifted from its intended position. Remove and reinsert the lens; if the problem continues, consult your eye care professional. Axis misalignment requires a  or fitting adjustment, not self-correction. Frequently Asked Questions — SofLens Toric 6 Pack What is the difference between SofLens Toric and SofLens 66 Toric? They are the same product. SofLens 66 Toric was the original name — the "66" referred to the 66% water content of the Alphafilcon A material. Bausch + Lomb rebranded to SofLens Toric, but the lens itself — material, design, and parameters — is unchanged. If your  reads SofLens 66 Toric, you can order SofLens Toric 6 Pack at contactlens365.com directly. How long does one SofLens Toric 6 Pack last? Each lens is worn for 30 days, so one 6-lens box provides a 6-month supply for one eye. For both eyes, you need two boxes — giving a 3-month combined supply for both eyes. Two boxes per order is the most common purchase for full binocular coverage. What cylinder powers are available in SofLens Toric? Five cylinder powers are available: –0.75 D, –1.25 D, –1.75 D, –2.25 D, and –2.75 D. Axes are available in 10° increments from 10° to 180° — 18 axis positions in total. Spherical power range is –9.00 to +6.00 D. Always order the exact parameters specified on your . Why is my vision slightly blurry immediately after inserting a SofLens Toric? Brief initial blur after insertion is normal for toric lenses. The lens needs a few blinks to rotate to the correct cylindrical axis on the eye. This usually resolves within seconds. If blurring persists beyond this settling period, or if vision fluctuates throughout the day, consult your eye care professional — this may indicate lens rotation or a fitting issue requiring a  adjustment. Is SofLens Toric suitable for dry eye wearers? The Alphafilcon A material's 66% water content provides a naturally moist initial feel that many wearers with mild dryness find comfortable. However, suitability for dry eye varies significantly between individuals. If you have dry eye, consult your eye care professional before choosing a contact lens — they can recommend the most appropriate lens type and wearing schedule for your specific condition. Can I wear SofLens Toric if I also need multifocal correction? The standard SofLens Toric corrects astigmatism but does not correct presbyopia. If you require both cylindrical and multifocal correction, speak to your eye care professional about options — which may include monovision fitting, a toric multifocal lens, or another design. The SofLens Multifocal is available for presbyopia without astigmatism. Which multipurpose solution should I use with SofLens Toric? SofLens Toric is compatible with all major multipurpose contact lens solutions. There is no single required product. Your eye care professional may recommend a specific solution based on your tear film chemistry and personal lens care habits — always follow their guidance. Can I swim or shower while wearing SofLens Toric? No. Remove all contact lenses before swimming, showering, or entering any body of water. Tap water, pool water, sea water, and hot tub water all carry micro-organisms — including Acanthamoeba — that can cause serious, sight-threatening eye infections on contact with a lens surface. } .suited-grid, .why-grid, .family-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; } .tech-row { flex-direction: column; } } Bausch + Lomb · ULTRA contactlens365.com ULTRA®6 Pack Bausch + Lomb's flagship monthly lens — MoistureSeal™ technology retains moisture for up to 16 hours, delivering consistent all-day comfort with every blink, every month. 6 lenses per box. ✦ Monthly Disposable 6 Lenses / Box MoistureSeal™ Technology Samfilcon A BC 8.5 mm · Dia 14.2 mm Monthly Wear Type 6 Lenses / Box 163 Dk/t O₂ Transmit. 46% Water Content ✓ 100% Genuine 📅 Expiry Date Checked 🚚 Fast Shipping 🔒 Secure Checkout ↩ Easy Returns Bausch + Lomb ULTRA® 6 Pack is Bausch + Lomb's cornerstone monthly lens — and one of the most comfort-rated monthly replacements available at contactlens365.com. Built on Samfilcon A silicone hydrogel with MoistureSeal™ technology, it is engineered to retain moisture for up to 16 hours without relying on wetting agents that deplete over the course of the day. The result is a monthly lens that performs as well at 6pm as it does at 8am. Order at contactlens365.com with  authenticity,  expiry dates, and secure payment options. What distinguishes the ULTRA® from its predecessors and many competitors is the integration of moisture retention directly into the Samfilcon A polymer matrix — not added as a surface coating that wears away with each blink and cleaning cycle. MoistureSeal™ technology binds a moisture-retaining agent throughout the lens material, so comfort levels remain consistent across the full 30-day replacement cycle, not just on day one. 💧 MoistureSeal™ Technology: Clinically demonstrated to retain moisture for up to 16 hours — independent of the lens care solution used. Comfort that lasts all day, every day, for the full month. Technical Specifications Parameter Value Material Samfilcon A (Silicone Hydrogel) Water Content 46% Base Curve (BC) 8.5 mm Diameter 14.2 mm Oxygen Transmissibility (Dk/t) 163 UV Protection None Power Range (SPH) –10.00 to +6.00 D Replacement Schedule Monthly (30 days) Pack Contents 6 Lenses Manufacturer Bausch + Lomb Key Technologies MoistureSeal™ A proprietary moisture-retention system built into the Samfilcon A polymer matrix itself — not a surface coating. Moisture levels remain consistent for up to 16 hours of wear regardless of the multipurpose solution used, and across the entire 30-day replacement cycle. Samfilcon A Bausch + Lomb's high-performance silicone hydrogel polymer. With Dk/t 163 and 46% water content, it delivers industry-leading oxygen transmissibility for a monthly lens while maintaining the natural, moist on-eye feel that wearers expect from a premium replacement schedule lens. The ULTRA® Lens Family The ULTRA® 6 Pack is the spherical foundation of a complete lens family — all sharing the same Samfilcon A material and MoistureSeal™ platform. Explore the full range at contactlens365.com. This Product ULTRA® Monthly Spherical monthly lens. Standard myopia and hyperopia correction. The original ULTRA® platform. Also Available ULTRA® for Astigmatism Monthly toric lens. Same MoistureSeal™ comfort with precise cylindrical correction for astigmatism. Also Available ULTRA® Multifocal Monthly multifocal lens. 3-Zone Progressive™ Design for presbyopia correction across all distances. Product Details The ULTRA® was developed in response to a well-documented challenge with silicone hydrogel monthly lenses: despite their oxygen performance, many users experience progressive discomfort as the wearing day extends — particularly in air-conditioned environments, during screen use, and in the second and third weeks of a replacement cycle when deposit accumulation begins to affect the lens surface. Samfilcon A with MoistureSeal™ approaches this problem at the material level. Rather than adding a hydrophilic surface coating that gradually wears away, the moisture-retaining agents are incorporated throughout the polymer itself. Clinical trials showed moisture retention of up to 16 hours — and consistent comfort across the full 30-day cycle — distinguishing the ULTRA® from coated silicone hydrogels that perform better in the first week than the third. At Dk/t 163, the ULTRA® is among the most oxygen-permeable monthly lenses available. The 46% water content places it at a balance point between high-oxygen silicone hydrogels (which can be drier) and higher-water content hydrogels (which have lower Dk/t). This balance is reflected in real-world comfort ratings that consistently place the ULTRA® at the top of monthly replacement category surveys. At contactlens365.com all available parameters are held in stock for prompt dispatch. Who Is It For? 💧 Dry Eye & Comfort-First Wearers MoistureSeal™ technology targets the root cause of end-of-day dryness — moisture depletion. Up to 16 hours of clinically demonstrated hydration without relying on rewetting drops. 💻 Screen-Heavy Professionals Reduced blink rate during screen work accelerates tear film evaporation. The ULTRA®'s moisture matrix helps maintain on-eye hydration through long working days without additional intervention. 🏃 Active Lifestyles High Dk/t and robust deposit resistance make the ULTRA® a reliable performer from desk to gym to outdoors — consistent comfort regardless of the demands of the day. 🌬️ Air-Conditioned Environments Air conditioning and low-humidity environments accelerate lens dehydration. The polymer-integrated moisture of Samfilcon A is significantly more resilient to environmental drying than surface-coated alternatives. 🔄 Monthly Comfort Upgraders If your current monthly lens becomes uncomfortable in week two or three, the ULTRA®'s full-cycle moisture consistency is a meaningful upgrade worth trialling with your eye care professional. 📋 Wide  Range –10.00 to +6.00 D covers the majority of myopic and hyperopic prescriptions. Also available in toric (astigmatism) and multifocal (presbyopia) versions across the ULTRA® family. How to Use & Care 1Wash your hands thoroughly with soap and water. Dry with a lint-free towel before handling any lens. 2Inspect before insertion — check for tears, discolouration, or deformation. The lens should form a smooth bowl shape. Never insert a damaged or inside-out lens. 3Insert gently — hold your lids open and place the lens onto the cornea. Blink to settle and check comfort and vision. 4Clean after every removal — rub each lens surface with multipurpose solution for at least 5 seconds per side, then rinse thoroughly. Always rub — even with "no-rub" labelled solutions. 5Store in fresh solution — empty the case completely and refill with fresh multipurpose solution every time. Never top up old solution with new. 6Maintain your lens case — rinse daily with solution (never tap water), wipe with a tissue, and leave face-down to air dry. Replace the case every 1–3 months. 7Replace every 30 days — note the opening date. Even with MoistureSeal™'s deposit-resistant matrix, no monthly lens should be worn beyond its replacement schedule. 8Remove before sleep unless your eye doctor has specifically  overnight wear. Always remove before swimming, showering, or using a hot tub. 💡 Maximising comfort: MoistureSeal™ is solution-independent — it works with any major multipurpose solution. However, your eye care professional may recommend a specific solution based on your individual tear chemistry. Always follow professional guidance on lens care products. Why Shop at contactlens365.com? ✅ 100% Authentic Every product sourced exclusively from authorised distributors — zero risk of  or expired lenses. 🚀 Fast Dispatch Orders placed before cut-off ship same day. Track your parcel in real time from order to doorstep. 🛡️ Secure Payments , debit card, and other secure payment methods accepted. Your data is always protected. 💬 Expert Support Our customer care team is available 7 days a week to help with parameter selection, orders, and returns. 🔄 Auto-Reorder Set up a subscription and receive your ULTRA® automatically every 3 months — never run out again. ↩ Hassle-Free Returns Not satisfied? Our straightforward returns and exchange policy has you covered. Frequently Asked Questions How should I store ULTRA® lenses? After each use, rub both surfaces with multipurpose contact lens solution for at least 5 seconds per side, rinse thoroughly, then store in a clean case with fresh solution. Replace solution and case regularly. Never use tap water, saline, or saliva. What makes MoistureSeal™ different from other moisture technologies? Most competing moisture technologies work by adding a hydrophilic surface coating to the lens. These coatings gradually wear away with blinking and cleaning — which is why many monthly lenses feel comfortable in week one but less so by week three. MoistureSeal™ integrates the moisture-retaining agent throughout the Samfilcon A polymer matrix itself, so comfort levels remain consistent across the full 30-day cycle. How long does one 6-pack last? A 6-lens box covers approximately 3 months for one eye (one lens per month). Two boxes — one per eye — provide a full 3-month supply for both eyes. Is the ULTRA® 6 Pack suitable for astigmatism or presbyopia? The standard spherical ULTRA® does not correct astigmatism or presbyopia. Bausch + Lomb offers the ULTRA® for Astigmatism (toric) and ULTRA® Multifocal for these needs — both available at contactlens365.com. Please consult your eye care professional for the appropriate version. Can I swim while wearing ULTRA® lenses? No. All contact lenses should be removed before swimming, showering, or entering a hot tub. Water exposure — including tap water — substantially raises the risk of serious eye infection. Which multipurpose solution works best with ULTRA®? MoistureSeal™ technology is clinically demonstrated to work independently of the lens care solution used — meaning ULTRA® performs well with all major multipurpose solutions. Your eye care professional may still recommend a specific solution based on your individual tear film chemistry and lens care habits. What is the current price? For the latest pricing and any active promotions, visit the ULTRA® 6 Pack product page at contactlens365.com. Subscribe to our newsletter to receive exclusive  alerts. Can I sleep in ULTRA® lenses? Unless your eye doctor has specifically  extended wear, do not sleep in contact lenses. Doing so significantly increases the risk of infection and corneal damage. bausch lomb ultra 6 pack ultra monthly contact lens buy bausch lomb ultra moistureseal monthly lens samfilcon a monthly lens bausch lomb monthly disposable contactlens365 bausch lomb ultra 6 pack specs ultra monthly reviews bc 8.5 mm monthly lens 14.2 mm diameter monthly lens dk 163 monthly lens silicone hydrogel monthly lens 16 hour moisture monthly lens contact lenses online fast shipping monthly lenses genuine bausch lomb lenses contactlens365 monthly lens for dry eyes best monthly comfort lens This product page is prepared by contactlens365.com. Bausch + Lomb, ULTRA, MoistureSeal, and Samfilcon A are trademarks of Bausch & Lomb Incorporated or its affiliates. Always consult a qualified eye care professional before using contact lenses.

Built on the same Samfilcon A silicone hydrogel with Dk/t 163 and 46% water content as the ULTRA® Monthly, it delivers outstanding all-day moisture retention and oxygen supply in a precision toric design engineered to stay correctly oriented throughout the day. Order at contactlens365.com with  authenticity,  expiry dates, and secure payment options. Astigmatism affects a significant proportion of contact lens wearers, yet toric lenses have historically lagged behind spherical equivalents in comfort. The ULTRA® for Astigmatism bridges this gap — pairing a clinically tested toric design with the same material science that made the ULTRA® Monthly one of the most comfort-rated monthly lenses on the market. The result is a toric lens that wearers describe as comparable in comfort to a standard spherical lens, while delivering precise cylindrical correction across the full replacement cycle. 💧 MoistureSeal™ technology retains moisture for up to 16 hours — the same performance benchmark as the ULTRA® Monthly — now available in a toric format. Reliable vision correction and all-day comfort, no trade-off required. Technical Specifications Parameter Value Material Samfilcon A (Silicone Hydrogel) Water Content 46% Base Curve (BC) 8.5 mm Diameter 14.5 mm Oxygen Transmissibility (Dk/t) 163 UV Protection None Sphere (SPH) Range –9.00 to +6.00 D Cylinder (CYL) Range –0.75, –1.25, –1.75, –2.25 D Axis Range 10° – 180° (in 10° steps) Replacement Schedule Monthly (30 days) Pack Contents 6 Lenses Manufacturer Bausch + Lomb Key Technologies MoistureSeal™ Bausch + Lomb's proprietary moisture-retention technology locks hydration into the Samfilcon A matrix — not just a surface coating — delivering up to 16 hours of consistent comfort without depending on rewetting drops or supplementary wetting agents. Samfilcon A The same high-performance silicone hydrogel used across the entire ULTRA® lens family. With 46% water content and Dk/t 163, it provides one of the highest oxygen transmissibility values available in any monthly toric lens — protecting corneal health across the full 30-day cycle. Toric Stabilisation The ULTRA® for Astigmatism uses a proven toric stabilisation design that maintains consistent axis orientation throughout the day — including during physical activity, changes in gaze direction, and extended screen use when blink rate fluctuates. Understanding Your Toric  A toric lens  contains three parameters beyond what a standard spherical lens requires. All three must be entered correctly when ordering — confirm each value with your eye care professional before placing your order. SPH — Sphere Distance / Near Power Standard refractive correction for myopia (–) or hyperopia (+). Available range: –9.00 to +6.00 D. Plano (0.00) also available. CYL — Cylinder Astigmatism Power The power correcting irregular corneal curvature. Available values: –0.75, –1.25, –1.75, –2.25 D. Always a minus value on contact lens prescriptions. AXIS Orientation Angle The angle at which the cylindrical correction is positioned. Range: 10° to 180° in 10° increments. Maintained by the toric stabilisation design. BC & DIA Fit Parameters Base curve 8.5 mm, diameter 14.5 mm. The 14.5 mm diameter is slightly larger than many toric alternatives, supporting lens stability and coverage. 📋  Guide Your toric  will show values for both eyes — right eye as OD, left eye as OS. Each eye will have its own SPH, CYL, and AXIS values. When ordering at contactlens365.com, enter each eye's parameters separately as shown on your . If your CYL value or axis falls between available options, your eye care professional will advise the most appropriate substitute — never self-substitute toric parameters. What Is Astigmatism? Astigmatism occurs when the cornea or natural lens of the eye is shaped more like a rugby ball than a sphere — causing light to focus at two different points rather than one, resulting in blurred or distorted vision at all distances. It is one of the most common refractive conditions and cannot be corrected by a standard spherical lens. A toric design incorporates both a spherical power and a cylindrical correction at a precise axis angle — and must stay in that orientation on the eye throughout wear. Who Is It For? 👁️ Astigmatism Wearers Designed specifically for astigmatism correction in a premium monthly format — precise cylindrical correction with ULTRA®-grade comfort throughout the full replacement cycle. 💧 Dry Eye & Comfort-First Wearers MoistureSeal™ technology provides sustained moisture retention for up to 16 hours — particularly valuable for toric wearers who have experienced dryness or discomfort in other monthly toric lenses. 💻 Screen-Heavy Professionals High Dk/t and sustained moisture support long days of screen work — the lens maintains comfort and optical stability even when blink rate drops during extended focus tasks. 🏃 Active Lifestyles The toric stabilisation design maintains correct axis orientation during physical activity, sport, and movement — reliable vision correction regardless of what your day involves. 🔄 ULTRA® Monthly Wearers with Astigmatism Already on the ULTRA® Monthly and happy with the comfort? The ULTRA® for Astigmatism uses the same Samfilcon A material and MoistureSeal™ — familiar comfort, now with precise toric correction. 📋 Toric Comfort Upgraders If your current monthly toric lens leaves you with end-of-day dryness or variable vision, the ULTRA® for Astigmatism offers a meaningfully different comfort and moisture experience. How to Use & Care 1Wash your hands thoroughly with soap and water. Dry with a lint-free towel before handling any lens. 2Inspect before insertion — check for tears, discolouration, or deformation. The lens should form a smooth bowl shape. An inside-out toric lens will not sit at the correct axis — never insert it. 3Insert gently — hold lids open and place the lens onto the cornea. Blink naturally to settle. Allow a moment — toric lenses may take one or two extra blinks to orientate correctly after insertion. 4Check vision after settling. If vision remains blurred or unstable after several blinks, remove, inspect, and reinsert. Persistent blurring may indicate the lens needs repositioning — consult your eye doctor if it continues. 5Clean after every removal — rub each lens surface with multipurpose solution for at least 5 seconds per side, rinse thoroughly, then store in fresh solution. The rubbing step is essential for toric lenses — it removes deposits that can affect both comfort and optical stability. 6Store in fresh solution — always empty the case and refill completely with fresh multipurpose solution. Never top up old solution with new. 7Replace every 30 days — note the date when you open a new lens. Protein and lipid deposits on a toric lens affect both comfort and how well the stabilisation design functions. Always follow the replacement schedule. 8Remove before sleep and before swimming, showering, or using a hot tub. Never use tap water to rinse or store lenses. 💡 Toric lens tip: If vision becomes transiently blurred during the day — particularly after prolonged downward gaze or screen work — blink firmly a few times to allow the stabilisation design to re-seat the lens. If blurring is frequent or persistent, consult your eye care professional to confirm the lens is fitting correctly. Why Shop at contactlens365.com? ✅ 100% Authentic Every product sourced exclusively from authorised distributors — zero risk of  or expired lenses. 🚀 Fast Dispatch Orders placed before cut-off ship same day. Track your parcel in real time from order to doorstep. 🛡️ Secure Payments , debit card, and other secure payment methods accepted. Your data is always protected. 💬 Expert Support Our team is available 7 days a week — including help with CYL/axis parameter selection and availability queries. 🔄 Auto-Reorder Set up a subscription and receive your ULTRA® for Astigmatism automatically every 3 months — never run out again. ↩ Hassle-Free Returns Not satisfied? Our straightforward returns and exchange policy has you covered. Frequently Asked Questions How should I store ULTRA® for Astigmatism lenses? After each use, rub both surfaces with multipurpose contact lens solution for at least 5 seconds per side, rinse thoroughly, and store in a clean lens case filled with fresh solution. Never use tap water, saline, or saliva to clean or store lenses. How is a toric  different from a regular one? A toric  includes Cylinder (CYL) and Axis values in addition to the standard Sphere (SPH). These three parameters together define the full correction for astigmatism. All three values are needed to order correctly — check your  with your eye care professional before ordering. How long does one 6-pack last? A 6-lens box covers approximately 3 months for one eye (one lens per month). Two boxes — one per eye — provide 3 months of full daily wear for both eyes. What if my CYL or axis value isn't available? Available CYL values are –0.75, –1.25, –1.75, and –2.25 D. Axis is available from 10° to 180° in 10° steps. If your  falls between these values, your eye care professional will recommend the closest appropriate substitute. Never self-substitute toric parameters. Is ULTRA® for Astigmatism the same material as ULTRA® Monthly? Yes — both use Samfilcon A silicone hydrogel with MoistureSeal™ technology. The ULTRA® for Astigmatism adds a toric optical design and stabilisation geometry for astigmatism correction. The comfort and oxygen performance are identical. Can I swim while wearing these lenses? No. All contact lenses should be removed before swimming, showering, or entering a hot tub. Water exposure — including tap water — substantially raises the risk of serious eye infection. What is the current price? For the latest pricing and any active promotions, visit the product page at contactlens365.com. Subscribe to our newsletter to receive exclusive  alerts. Can I sleep in ULTRA® for Astigmatism lenses? Unless your eye doctor has specifically  extended wear, do not sleep in contact lenses. } } Alcon · Air Optix contactlens365.com Air Optix® Colors2 Pack Alcon's premium monthly colour contact lens — 12 vibrant shades on a breathable silicone hydrogel platform. Available with and without  correction for a fresh, natural-looking colour change. ✦ Monthly Colour Lens 12 Colours 2 Lenses / Box With & Without  Lotrafilcon B BC 8.6 mm · Dia 14.2 mm Monthly Wear Type 12 Colours 138 Dk/t O₂ Transmit. 33% Water Content ✓ 100% Genuine 📅 Expiry Date Checked 🚚 Fast Shipping 🔒 Secure Checkout ↩ Easy Returns Alcon Air Optix® Colors 2 Pack is the world's most popular monthly colour contact lens — available at contactlens365.com in all 12 shades, with and without . Built on the same Lotrafilcon B silicone hydrogel used in the original Air Optix® lens platform, it delivers a genuinely breathable colour lens experience that doesn't force you to choose between appearance and eye health. Order at contactlens365.com with  authenticity,  expiry dates, and secure payment options. What sets Air Optix® Colors apart from cheaper colour lens alternatives is the quality of both the optics and the material. The 3-in-1 colour technology creates a natural-looking iris pattern — a dark outer ring, a blended colour zone, and a clear central area for unobstructed vision — while the Lotrafilcon B platform provides Dk/t 138 oxygen transmissibility, far exceeding the minimum safe threshold even for colour lenses, which are typically thicker than clear alternatives. ✨ Air Optix® Colors is the only monthly colour lens built on a clinically proven, high-Dk/t silicone hydrogel platform. The same breathable material used in millions of clear lens fittings — now available in 12 shades. Technical Specifications Parameter Value Material Lotrafilcon B (Silicone Hydrogel) Water Content 33% Base Curve (BC) 8.6 mm Diameter 14.2 mm Oxygen Transmissibility (Dk/t) 138 UV Protection None Power Range (SPH) –10.00 to +6.00 D (plano 0.00 also available) Colour Technology 3-in-1 Colour Design Available Colours 12 shades Replacement Schedule Monthly (30 days) Pack Contents 2 Lenses (1 pair) Manufacturer Alcon Available Colours Air Optix® Colors is available in 12 carefully developed shades — from subtle enhancements to dramatic transformations. Each colour uses Alcon's 3-in-1 design: a defining outer ring, a natural-blended iris pattern, and a clear optical centre for uncompromised vision quality. Blue Classic cool blue Brilliant Blue Vivid deep blue Blue Turquoise Fresh aqua tone Brown Warm natural brown Chestnut Rich dark brown Gray Soft silver-grey Green Natural green Green Hazel Earthy olive blend Hazel Brown-gold blend Honey Warm amber glow Pure Hazel Deeper hazel tone Sterling Gray Cool blue-grey Key Technology 3-in-1 Colour Design Each lens features three distinct zones: a dark outer limbal ring for definition and depth, a multi-tonal iris pattern for natural blending, and a completely clear central optical zone for undistorted vision. The result looks natural up close and from a distance. Lotrafilcon B Alcon's proven silicone hydrogel polymer from the Air Optix® platform. With Dk/t 138 and 33% water content, it provides oxygen-rich, comfortable wear — a significant step above the low-Dk hydrogel materials used in many cheaper colour lenses. SmartShield® Technology A thin plasma coating on the lens surface helps resist deposit build-up from lipids and proteins in the tear film — keeping the lens surface clean and comfortable throughout the full 30-day replacement cycle. Product Details Colour contact lenses are inherently more complex to manufacture than clear lenses — the pigment layer adds thickness and can affect oxygen flow, deposit resistance, and on-eye comfort. Air Optix® Colors addresses this by building its colour design on the Lotrafilcon B platform, where the underlying silicone hydrogel provides enough oxygen transmissibility to compensate for the additional lens structure. The 3-in-1 colour design places tinted layers between thin silicone hydrogel layers — not on the outer surface. This sandwiching technique ensures the pigments don't come into direct contact with the eye or tear film, which is both safer and better for colour longevity over the wearing month. Air Optix® Colors is available with a plano (0.00) power option, making it accessible to people with naturally good vision who simply want to change their eye colour. For  wearers, the range of –10.00 to +6.00 D covers the majority of myopic and hyperopic corrections. At contactlens365.com, all 12 colours are held in stock across available powers for prompt dispatch. Will the Colour Show on Dark Eyes? This is the most common question about colour contacts. Air Optix® Colors uses an opaque colour layer — not a tint — which means the colours are clearly visible even on naturally dark brown or black irises. Lighter colours such as Blue, Gray, and Green tend to show a more subtle transformation on dark eyes, while Honey, Hazel, and Brown create warmer, more natural-looking results. Your eye care professional or our support team can advise on which shades tend to work best for your natural eye colour. Who Is It For? ✨ Fashion & Style Enthusiasts Change your eye colour to match your mood, outfit, or occasion. Air Optix® Colors offers 12 shades for a full spectrum of looks — from everyday natural to bold and dramatic. 👁️  Wearers No compromise needed — correct your vision and change your eye colour simultaneously. Available from –10.00 to +6.00 D across all 12 shades. 🎭 Special Occasions Weddings, events, photo shoots, or cosplay — the monthly format means you can keep a pair on hand for whenever a specific look is needed. 🌿 Natural Enhancement Seekers Not looking for a dramatic change? Shades like Hazel, Honey, and Green Hazel subtly enhance natural eye colour rather than replacing it completely. 👓 Plano (No ) Wearers Perfect vision doesn't mean you can't wear contact lenses. The 0.00 plano option makes Air Optix® Colors accessible to anyone — no  needed, just an eye doctor fitting. 🔄 Monthly Routine Users The monthly replacement schedule makes Air Optix® Colors easy to integrate into a regular lens routine — replace once a month, just like any other monthly lens. ⚠️ Important safety note: All contact lenses — including colour lenses — require a valid  and professional fitting from a licensed eye care professional. Never purchase or share contact lenses without a fitting, even for plano (non-corrective) use. Ill-fitting colour lenses are a leading cause of preventable eye infections and corneal damage. How to Use & Care 1Wash your hands thoroughly with soap and water. Dry with a lint-free towel before handling any lens. 2Inspect before insertion — check for tears, discolouration, or deformation. Confirm correct orientation (bowl shape). Never insert a damaged or inside-out lens. 3Insert gently — hold lids open and place the lens onto the cornea. Blink to settle. The colour zone should be well-centred over the iris. 4Clean after every removal — rub each lens surface with multipurpose solution for at least 5 seconds per side, then rinse thoroughly. Never skip the rubbing step, even with "no-rub" solutions. 5Store in fresh solution — always empty the case completely and refill with fresh multipurpose solution before storing. Never top up old solution with new. 6Maintain your lens case — rinse daily with solution (never tap water), wipe clean with a tissue, and air dry face-down. Replace the case every 1–3 months. 7Replace every 30 days — colour lenses accumulate deposits just like clear lenses. Always follow the replacement schedule regardless of how the lens looks or feels. 8Remove before sleep and before swimming, showering, or using a hot tub. Never share contact lenses with anyone — ever. Why Shop at contactlens365.com? ✅ 100% Authentic Every product sourced exclusively from authorised distributors — zero risk of  lenses, which are particularly dangerous in colour lens products. 🎨 All 12 Colours In Stock All shades across available powers held in stock for prompt dispatch — no waiting for back-orders on popular colours. 🚀 Fast Dispatch Orders placed before cut-off ship same day. Track your parcel in real time from order to doorstep. 🛡️ Secure Payments , debit card, and other secure methods accepted. Your data is always protected. 💬 Expert Support Our team is available 7 days a week — including advice on which colours work best for different natural eye colours. ↩ Hassle-Free Returns Not the right shade? Our straightforward returns and exchange policy has you covered. Frequently Asked Questions Do I need a  for Air Optix® Colors? Yes — all contact lenses, including colour and plano (non-corrective) lenses, legally require a valid contact lens  from a licensed eye care professional in most countries. A  confirms your base curve, diameter, and whether your eyes are healthy enough for lens wear. Never purchase contact lenses without a professional fitting. Will Air Optix® Colors show on dark eyes? Yes. Air Optix® Colors uses an opaque colour layer — not just a tint — so the colour is visible even on naturally dark brown or black irises. Cooler shades like Blue and Gray create a more dramatic contrast on dark eyes, while Honey, Hazel, and Brown deliver a warmer, more natural-looking result. Results vary between individuals based on natural eye colour and iris pattern. Can I get Air Optix® Colors without a  (plano)? Yes, the plano (0.00) option is available for wearers who don't need vision correction. However, a contact lens fitting from an eye care professional is still required — plano lenses need the same fitting process as  lenses to ensure safety and comfort. How should I store Air Optix® Colors? After each day of wear, clean by rubbing with multipurpose contact lens solution for at least 5 seconds per surface, rinse thoroughly, then store in a clean case with fresh solution. Replace the solution and case regularly. Never use tap water or saliva. How long does one 2-pack last? A 2-lens box contains one pair (one for each eye). With monthly replacement, one box covers approximately one month of wear. For a 3-month supply, you would need 3 boxes. Are colour contact lenses safe? Yes — when properly fitted, prescribed, and cared for. Air Optix® Colors is manufactured by Alcon to the same safety standards as their clear lens range. The risks associated with colour lenses typically arise from  products, lenses bought without a fitting, improper hygiene, or exceeding the replacement schedule — all of which are easily avoided. What is the current price? For the latest pricing and any active promotions, visit the Air Optix® Colors product page at contactlens365.com. Subscribe to our newsletter to receive exclusive  alerts. Can I sleep in Air Optix® Colors? No. Air Optix® Colors is  for daily wear only — remove before sleep. Never sleep in colour contact lenses. The "XR" designation stands for eXtended Range: this lens is engineered to correct prescriptions as strong as –20.00 D in myopia and up to +20.00 D in hyperopia, making it one of the most accessible premium monthly options available to high- wearers worldwide. Built on the same Comfilcon A silicone hydrogel and Aquaform® Technology as the standard Biofinity®, the XR version delivers the same outstanding comfort  — including an industry-leading Dk/t of 160 — in a lens geometry designed to maintain optical quality at extreme powers. Order at contactlens365.com with  authenticity,  expiry dates, and secure payment options. 🔬 Biofinity® XR extends the Biofinity® power range to –20.00 D (myopia) and +20.00 D (hyperopia) — far beyond the –12.00 to +8.00 D typical of standard monthly lenses. It is the go-to premium monthly option for wearers who have historically been limited to custom or rigid lenses. Technical Specifications Parameter Value Material Comfilcon A (Silicone Hydrogel) Water Content 48% Base Curve (BC) 8.6 mm Diameter 14.0 mm Oxygen Transmissibility (Dk/t) 160 UV Protection None Myopia Range (SPH –) –0.50 to –20.00 D Hyperopia Range (SPH +) +0.50 to +20.00 D Replacement Schedule Monthly (30 days) Pack Contents 6 Lenses Manufacturer CooperVision Extended Power Range The defining feature of Biofinity® XR is its extraordinary  coverage. While most monthly lenses stop at –12.00 or –10.00 D, the XR continues all the way to –20.00 D — providing a comfortable, breathable monthly option for wearers who have often been told their  is "too strong" for standard soft lenses. Available Power Ranges Myopia (Short-Sightedness) –0.50 D to –20.00 D Minus (–) values. In 0.25 D steps up to –6.00 D, then 0.50 D steps beyond. Hyperopia (Long-Sightedness) +0.50 D to +20.00 D Plus (+) values. In 0.25 D steps up to +6.00 D, then 0.50 D steps beyond. Base Curve 8.6 mm Single BC accommodates the majority of corneal geometries. Diameter 14.0 mm Slightly smaller than average — aids centration at high powers. Key Technology Aquaform® Technology CooperVision's proprietary approach to silicone hydrogel formulation — naturally incorporating water throughout the Comfilcon A matrix without surface treatments or coatings. The result is a lens that stays naturally moist throughout the full day and the full month of wear. Comfilcon A CooperVision's silicone hydrogel polymer with an industry-leading Dk/t of 160 — among the highest of any monthly replacement lens. Combined with 48% water content, it delivers exceptional oxygen supply to the cornea without sacrificing on-eye comfort. XR Optical Design High-power lenses become progressively thicker or thinner at the edges as  strength increases, which can impair comfort and oxygen flow. Biofinity® XR's optic design manages lens thickness distribution to maintain comfort and Dk/t even at powers well beyond –12.00 D. Product Details High- lenses present a genuine engineering challenge. As minus power increases, lenses grow thicker at the edges — creating discomfort, reducing oxygen supply at the periphery, and making centration harder. Conversely, high-plus lenses become thick at the centre. The Biofinity® XR addresses these challenges through refined lens geometry that manages edge and centre thickness distribution at extreme powers, keeping the Dk/t at a clinically excellent 160 across the entire range. The 14.0 mm diameter is intentionally slightly smaller than the 14.2 mm standard Biofinity® — this design choice helps maintain lens stability and centration as prescriptions increase, where edge mass distribution can otherwise cause the lens to shift during wear. Your eye care professional will confirm whether Biofinity® XR is appropriate for your corneal profile and . Aquaform® Technology means no surface coatings or wetting agents are needed to achieve the lens's moisture performance — water is an intrinsic part of the Comfilcon A material structure itself. This makes the lens naturally resistant to dehydration over both the wearing day and the 30-day replacement cycle, and means deposit accumulation on the surface is less aggressive than with coated alternatives. Who Is It For? 🔭 High Myopia Wearers Prescriptions from –12.50 D to –20.00 D that fall outside the range of standard monthly lenses. Biofinity® XR provides a comfortable, breathable monthly option where few exist. 🔍 High Hyperopia Wearers Strong plus prescriptions beyond +8.00 D — including those who have previously only been able to wear glasses or rigid lenses — can now access a premium monthly silicone hydrogel option. ✅ Standard Range Wearers Too Biofinity® XR is also available across the standard power range (–0.50 to –6.00 D / +0.50 to +6.00 D), meaning wearers who prefer its geometry can use it regardless of  strength. 💻 Screen & Demanding Use Dk/t 160 and Aquaform® moisture retention support long wearing days — making Biofinity® XR suitable for professionals and active users across all  levels. 🔄 Custom Lens Upgraders Wearers previously reliant on rigid gas-permeable or custom soft lenses for high prescriptions may find Biofinity® XR offers comparable correction with significantly better comfort and convenience. 💰 Value-Conscious High Rx Wearers Custom lenses are expensive and slow to replace. Biofinity® XR offers a stock monthly lens across most of the extended range — faster delivery and significantly lower per-lens cost. How to Use & Care 1Wash your hands thoroughly with soap and water. Dry with a lint-free towel before handling any lens. 2Inspect before insertion — check for tears, discolouration, or deformation. Confirm the lens forms a bowl shape, not a saucer. Never insert a damaged or inside-out lens. 3Insert gently — hold your lids open and place the lens onto the cornea. Blink to settle. If vision or comfort is poor after several blinks, remove and recheck. 4Clean after every removal — rub each lens surface with multipurpose solution for at least 5 seconds per side, then rinse thoroughly. Rubbing is recommended even with "no-rub" solutions. 5Store in fresh solution — always empty the lens case completely and refill with fresh multipurpose solution. Never top up old solution. 6Maintain your lens case — rinse daily with solution (never tap water), wipe with a clean tissue, and leave face-down to air dry. Replace the case every 1–3 months. 7Replace every 30 days — note the date when you open a new lens. Never extend beyond the replacement schedule, regardless of how the lens feels. 8Remove before sleep unless your eye doctor has specifically  overnight wear. Always remove before swimming, showering, or using a hot tub. ⚠️ High-power lens note: At extreme prescriptions, lenses have asymmetric edge profiles that may feel slightly different during insertion compared to standard-power lenses. Allow a brief settling period after blinking — this is normal. If vision is consistently blurred or unstable, consult your eye care professional to confirm correct base curve and fitting.
