Your data is always protected. 💬 Expert Support Our team is available 7 days a week — including guidance on high- parameter selection and availability. 🔄 Auto-Reorder Set up a subscription and receive your Biofinity® XR automatically every 3 months — never run out again. ↩ Hassle-Free Returns Not satisfied? Our straightforward returns and exchange policy has you covered. Frequently Asked Questions What does "XR" mean in Biofinity® XR? XR stands for eXtended Range. It refers to the significantly wider  range this lens covers compared to the standard Biofinity® — up to –20.00 D for myopia and +20.00 D for hyperopia, versus the –6.00 to +4.00 D of the standard version. How should I store Biofinity® XR lenses? After each use, clean with multipurpose contact lens solution (rub for at least 5 seconds per surface), rinse thoroughly, and store in a clean lens case filled with fresh solution. Never use tap water, saline, or saliva to clean or store lenses. Is Biofinity® XR the same material as standard Biofinity®? Yes. Biofinity® XR uses the same Comfilcon A silicone hydrogel with Aquaform® Technology as the standard Biofinity®. The difference is the extended power range and the refined lens geometry designed to maintain comfort and optical quality at extreme prescriptions. How long does one 6-pack last? A 6-lens box covers approximately 3 months for one eye (one lens per month, replaced every 30 days). Two boxes — one per eye — provide 3 months of full daily wear in both eyes. Is Biofinity® XR available for astigmatism? Biofinity® XR Toric is available separately for wearers who have both high prescriptions and astigmatism. Please consult your eye care professional for the appropriate toric  parameters. Can I swim while wearing Biofinity® XR? No. All contact lenses should be removed before swimming, showering, or entering a hot tub. Water exposure — including tap water — substantially raises the risk of serious eye infection. What is the current price? For the latest pricing and any active promotions, visit the Biofinity® XR product page at contactlens365.com. Subscribe to our newsletter to receive exclusive  alerts. Can I sleep in Biofinity® XR lenses? Biofinity® XR is  for up to 6 nights / 7 days of continuous wear in some markets when prescribed by an eye care professional for extended wear. However, extended wear significantly increases infection risk. Technical Specifications Parameter Value Material Senofilcon A (Silicone Hydrogel) Water Content 38% Base Curve (BC) 8.4 mm and 8.8 mm Diameter 14.3 mm Oxygen Transmissibility (Dk/t) 121 UV Protection Class 1 — UVA 99.9%, UVB 100% Blue-Violet Light Filter OptiBlue™ (~400–450 nm) Power Range (SPH) –12.00 to +8.00 D Replacement Schedule Daily Disposable Pack Contents 30 Lenses Manufacturer Johnson & Johnson Vision Key Technologies TearStable™ Stabilises the tear film on the lens surface for consistent optical clarity and comfort throughout the day — particularly effective during prolonged screen use when natural blink rate decreases and tear film is most vulnerable. OptiBlue™ A built-in blue-violet light filter (~400–450 nm) that reduces exposure from digital screens and artificial lighting without altering colour perception — helping to reduce the visual demands of a screen-heavy lifestyle. Class 1 UV Blocks 99.9% of UVA and 100% of UVB rays — the highest UV protection class available in any contact lens, providing meaningful daily defence against solar radiation alongside your regular eyewear. 30 Pack or 90 Pack? The ACUVUE® OASYS MAX 1-Day is available in both a 30-lens and a 90-lens box. Same lens, same technology — choose the format that suits your ordering preference. This Product 30 Pack — 1 Month Supply Ideal for first-time buyers trying the lens, or wearers who prefer monthly ordering and flexibility. Lower upfront cost per order. Also Available 90 Pack — 3 Month Supply Better value per lens for committed wearers. Fewer reorders, less packaging, and convenient if you use auto-reorder. Also available at contactlens365.com. Product Details The ACUVUE® OASYS MAX 1-Day is built on Senofilcon A — a well-established silicone hydrogel that has been continuously refined across the ACUVUE® OASYS platform for over a decade. At Dk/t 121, oxygen transmissibility exceeds the safe daily wear threshold by a significant margin, ensuring the cornea remains well-supplied even during the longest wearing days. Two base curve options — 8.4 mm and 8.8 mm — accommodate a broader range of corneal geometries than most daily lenses, which typically offer only a single base curve. Your eye care professional will confirm which base curve is appropriate for your eye shape. The 14.3 mm diameter and the optical quality of Senofilcon A together provide excellent centration and consistent visual acuity across all gaze directions. The power range of –12.00 to +8.00 D is one of the widest available in a daily lens format, making the ACUVUE® OASYS MAX 1-Day accessible for wearers with high myopia or hyperopia who might otherwise have been limited to monthly replacement lenses. At contactlens365.com, all parameters are held in stock for prompt dispatch. Who Is It For? 💻 Screen-Heavy Professionals TearStable™ and OptiBlue™ work together to address the two main challenges of digital work — tear film disruption and blue-violet light exposure — simultaneously. 👁️ Dry Eye Sufferers TearStable™ technology actively stabilises the tear film on the lens surface, making the OASYS MAX 1-Day a strong option for those who experience dryness with other daily lenses. ☀️ UV-Conscious Wearers Class 1 UV — 99.9% UVA and 100% UVB blocked — provides the maximum UV protection available in a contact lens every single day without any extra steps. 🏃 Active Lifestyles Daily disposables are the most hygienic and convenient format for sport and active use — no cleaning, no storage, and a fresh lens every day. ✈️ Frequent Travellers No solution to carry through airport security. The 30-pack covers a full month per eye with zero maintenance overhead — ideal for travel. 🌟 First-Time Lens Wearers The 30-pack is a practical starting point for anyone new to the OASYS MAX platform — a full month's trial with no long-term stock commitment before moving to the 90-pack. How to Use 1Wash your hands thoroughly with soap and water. Dry with a lint-free towel before handling any lens. 2Open the blister carefully — peel back the foil without squeezing the pack or spilling the storage solution. 3Check orientation — the lens should form a smooth bowl shape on your fingertip. If the edges flare outward, it is inside-out. Never insert an inside-out lens. 4Insert the lens by holding your upper and lower lids open and gently placing the lens onto the cornea. Release your lids and blink gently to settle. 5Check comfort and vision. Blurred vision or discomfort after blinking? Remove, inspect for damage, and reinsert. Persistent issues — stop wearing and consult your eye doctor. 6No solution needed. As a daily disposable, no lens case or cleaning solution is required. The blister solution can briefly keep the lens moist if you temporarily remove it — it is not a cleaning agent. 7Remove before sleep unless your eye doctor has specifically  overnight wear. Always remove before swimming, showering, or using a hot tub. 8Discard after one day. Pull your lower lid down, slide the lens off the cornea, and pinch out gently. Never reuse a daily disposable lens. 💡 Screen use tip: Blink frequency drops significantly during screen work. Apply the 20-20-20 rule — every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds. Ask your eye doctor about compatible lubricating drops if additional support is needed. Our straightforward returns and exchange policy has you covered. Frequently Asked Questions Do I need lens solution for the ACUVUE® OASYS MAX 1-Day? No. As a daily disposable lens, no cleaning solution or storage case is required. Simply open a fresh blister each morning and discard the lens at the end of the day. What is the difference between the 30 Pack and the 90 Pack? The lens is identical in both packs — same material, same technologies, same specifications. The 30-pack provides one month's supply per eye and suits those who prefer monthly ordering or want to trial the lens first. The 90-pack provides three months per eye and offers better value per lens for committed wearers. Both are available at contactlens365.com. How long does one 30-pack last? A 30-lens box covers approximately one month for one eye. Two boxes — one per eye — provide a full month's supply for both eyes. What does OptiBlue™ actually do? OptiBlue™ is a blue-violet light filter built into the lens material that reduces transmission of light in the ~400–450 nm wavelength range — the portion most associated with digital screen glare and potential visual fatigue. It does not tint the lens or alter colour perception during normal use. Is the ACUVUE® OASYS MAX 1-Day suitable for astigmatism? The standard spherical version does not correct astigmatism. The ACUVUE® OASYS MAX 1-Day for Astigmatism (toric version) is available separately — please consult your eye care professional for the correct toric . Can I swim while wearing these lenses? No. All contact lenses should be removed before swimming, showering, or entering a hot tub. Water exposure — including tap water — substantially raises the risk of serious eye infection. OpticAlign™ Design A precision multifocal optical design that aligns distance, intermediate, and near zones to the pupil — delivering reliable vision at every distance regardless of lighting. Product Details The Acuvue Oasys Max 1-Day Multifocal is engineered to meet every standard required by modern eye science. The 8.4 mm base curve and 14.3 mm diameter ensure a secure, comfortable fit across a wide range of corneal shapes. At Dk/t 121, oxygen transmissibility is well above the threshold required for healthy daily wear, keeping the cornea supplied with the oxygen it needs throughout the day. The 38% water content in Senofilcon A is carefully calibrated — balanced to provide a natural on-eye feel without drawing moisture from the tear film. TearStable™ technology builds on this foundation, actively stabilising the lens-tear film interface for sustained sharp vision even during the demanding visual tasks of modern life. Class 1 UV protection (UVA 99.9%, UVB 100%) is the highest protection class available in contact lenses, offering meaningful daily defence against solar radiation. Note that UV-blocking contact lenses are not a substitute for UV-protective sunglasses, which provide broader coverage around the eye. ADD Parameter & Presbyopia Guide Multifocal lenses integrate multiple optical powers into a single lens for simultaneous focus at near, intermediate, and distance ranges. Beyond the spherical power (SPH), the Addition (ADD) value is the most important parameter for presbyopia correction. What Is the ADD Value? The ADD represents the extra plus power required — on top of your distance correction — to allow clear near vision. As the eye's natural lens loses flexibility with age, the required ADD increases. ADD values typically range from +0.75 D to +3.00 D and are measured by your eye care professional. Available ADD Options LOW +0.75 D — +1.25 D For early-stage presbyopia. Typically suited to those just beginning to notice difficulty with small print or sustained near tasks. MID +1.25 D — +1.75 D For moderate presbyopia. Balances near and intermediate correction for everyday reading, screen use, and distance activities. HIGH +1.75 D — +2.50 D For advanced presbyopia. Provides strong near correction while maintaining useful distance and intermediate vision through OpticAlign™ Design. The appropriate ADD tier is determined by your eye care professional based on your current near vision needs. Spherical Power (SPH) Range The Acuvue Oasys Max 1-Day Multifocal is available from –10.00 to +6.00 D. Minus (–) values correct myopia; plus (+) values correct hyperopia. A power of 0.00 (plano) combined with an ADD value is used for pure presbyopia correction. Optical Design — OpticAlign™ OpticAlign™ Design uses a simultaneous vision approach with concentrically optimised zones for distance, intermediate, and near. The brain naturally selects the sharpest image through neural adaptation — a process that typically completes within one to two weeks of first wear. Adaptation Period Adapting to a multifocal lens differs from adapting to a single-vision lens. During the first one to two weeks, mild visual fluctuation or a slight awareness of the lens is entirely normal as the brain learns to interpret the new optical system. Wear the lenses for as long as possible each day and always use both eyes together. If significant discomfort or blurred vision persists beyond two weeks, consult your eye care professional. 📋  Guide Your multifocal lens  will include an ADD, Near Add, or Reading Add value alongside your SPH. Both the right (OD) and left (OS) eyes have separate SPH values but typically share the same ADD. When ordering at contactlens365.com, enter each eye's values separately as shown on your . One daily lens covers every distance. 💻 Screen-Heavy Professionals TearStable™ technology combats digital eye fatigue, keeping vision sharp and eyes comfortable through the longest workday. ✈️ Active Adults & Travellers No solution, no case, no cleaning routine. Pop in a fresh lens each morning and discard at night — ideal for travel. ☀️ UV-Conscious Wearers Class 1 UV protection provides daily defence against 99.9% UVA and 100% UVB — the most comprehensive UV blocking available in a contact lens. 🚗 Drivers & Outdoor Enthusiasts OpticAlign™ Design delivers reliable distance clarity in variable light conditions — confident vision behind the wheel and outdoors. How to Use 1Wash your hands thoroughly with soap and water. Dry with a lint-free towel before touching any lens. 2Open the blister carefully — peel back the foil without squeezing the pack or spilling the storage solution. 3Check orientation — place the lens on your fingertip. If it forms a bowl shape, it is correctly oriented. If the edges flare outward (like a saucer), it is inside-out. Never insert an inside-out lens. 4Insert the lens by holding your upper and lower lids open, gently placing the lens onto the cornea, then releasing your lids. Blink gently to settle. 5Check comfort and vision. Any foreign body sensation or blurred vision? Remove, check for damage, and reinsert. Persistent issues — stop wearing and consult your eye doctor. 6No solution needed. As a daily disposable, no lens case or cleaning solution is required. If you temporarily remove the lens during the day, the remaining blister solution can keep it moist briefly — but do not use it as a cleaning agent. 7Remove before sleep — unless your eye doctor has specifically  overnight wear. Sleeping in lenses significantly raises the risk of corneal infection. 8Discard after one day. Pull your lower lid down, slide the lens down from the cornea, and pinch it out with your thumb and index finger. Dispose of used lenses — never reuse a daily lens. 💡 Screen use tip: Blinking frequency drops significantly during screen work, which can reduce comfort. Apply the 20-20-20 rule — every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds. If needed, ask your eye doctor about compatible lubricating eye drops to use alongside your lenses. A 90-lens box is also available for a 3-month supply per eye. Is the Acuvue Oasys Max 1-Day Multifocal suitable for astigmatism? The standard spherical version does not correct astigmatism. If you have astigmatism in addition to presbyopia, please consult your eye care professional — a toric multifocal alternative may be more appropriate. Can I swim while wearing these lenses? No. All contact lenses should be removed before swimming, showering, or entering a hot tub. Water exposure — including tap water — substantially raises the risk of serious eye infection. What is the current price? For the latest pricing and any active promotions, visit the Acuvue Oasys Max 1-Day Multifocal product page at contactlens365.com. Subscribe to our newsletter to receive exclusive  alerts. How do I place an order at contactlens365.com? On the product page, select your base curve, power (SPH), and ADD (Low / Mid / High) for each eye, then add to cart and complete the secure checkout. Your order will be dispatched promptly. Can I sleep in Acuvue Oasys Max 1-Day Multifocal lenses? Unless your eye doctor has specifically  extended wear, do not sleep in contact lenses. } .suited-grid, .why-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; } .tech-row { flex-direction: column; } } Bausch + Lomb contactlens365.com INFUSE® One-Day30 Pack Also known as ULTRA® One Day in select markets Bausch + Lomb's most advanced daily lens — ProBalance Technology™ infuses a unique blend of ingredients directly into the lens to actively support the natural tear film all day long. 30 lenses per box. ✦ Daily Disposable 30 Lenses / Box ProBalance Technology™ Kalifilcon A BC 8.6 mm · Dia 14.2 mm Daily Wear Type 30 Lenses / Box 156 Dk/t O₂ Transmit. 55% Water Content ℹ️ INFUSE® One-Day and ULTRA® One Day — same lens, different name. Bausch + Lomb markets this lens as INFUSE® One-Day in North America and several international markets, and as ULTRA® One Day in others including the UK and parts of Europe. The material (Kalifilcon A), technology (ProBalance Technology™), and all specifications are identical. Both names refer to the same product. ✓ 100% Genuine 📅 Expiry Date Checked 🚚 Fast Shipping 🔒 Secure Checkout ↩ Easy Returns Bausch + Lomb INFUSE® One-Day 30 Pack is Bausch + Lomb's premium daily silicone hydrogel lens, available at contactlens365.com. Where most daily lenses focus on passive moisture retention, the INFUSE® One-Day takes an active approach — ProBalance Technology™ incorporates a blend of naturally inspired ingredients directly into the lens matrix, designed to work in harmony with the natural lipid and aqueous layers of the tear film. The result is a daily lens that actively supports eye comfort, not just temporarily hydrates it. Built on Kalifilcon A — a proprietary silicone hydrogel with a high 55% water content and Dk/t of 156 — the INFUSE® One-Day delivers outstanding oxygen permeability while maintaining the on-eye feel that extended wearers demand. It is the only daily lens on the market with ProBalance Technology™, making it a genuinely distinct option in a crowded category. 🌿 ProBalance Technology™ contains a unique blend of ingredients — including phospholipids and electrolytes — designed to mimic key components of the natural tear film. Rather than simply coating the lens surface, they interact with tear chemistry to actively support lipid and aqueous layer stability throughout the day. Technical Specifications Parameter Value Material Kalifilcon A (Silicone Hydrogel) Water Content 55% Base Curve (BC) 8.6 mm Diameter 14.2 mm Oxygen Transmissibility (Dk/t) 156 UV Protection None Power Range (SPH) –12.00 to +8.00 D Replacement Schedule Daily Disposable Pack Contents 30 Lenses Also Known As ULTRA® One Day (select markets) Manufacturer Bausch + Lomb Key Technology ProBalance Technology™ A proprietary blend of phospholipids, electrolytes, and other tear-inspired ingredients infused directly into the lens material. These work in harmony with the natural tear film — supporting both the lipid and aqueous layers — rather than simply adding surface hydration that can diminish over time. Kalifilcon A Bausch + Lomb's proprietary silicone hydrogel — developed specifically for the INFUSE® One-Day platform. With 55% water content and Dk/t 156, it combines high oxygen delivery with a naturally moist feel that sets it apart from lower water-content daily lenses. Product Details The tear film is a multi-layer structure: an outer lipid layer that prevents evaporation, an aqueous (watery) middle layer that keeps the surface moist, and an innermost mucin layer that anchors the tear film to the eye. Most lens discomfort originates from disruption to this structure — either the lens surface repels water (hydrophobic silicone) or the lens draws water from the aqueous layer. ProBalance Technology™ addresses both mechanisms simultaneously. At Dk/t 156, the INFUSE® One-Day is one of the highest oxygen-transmitting daily lenses available — providing ample corneal oxygen supply even during the longest wearing days. Combined with 55% water content, the Kalifilcon A material feels noticeably more hydrated on-eye than many silicone hydrogel dailies that trade water content for oxygen performance. With a power range of –12.00 to +8.00 D and a single base curve of 8.6 mm, the INFUSE® One-Day accommodates a wide range of prescriptions and corneal geometries. At contactlens365.com, all available parameters are held in stock for prompt dispatch. Always confirm your current  with your eye care professional before ordering. Who Is It For? 💧 Tear Film Sensitivity ProBalance Technology™ actively supports natural tear film chemistry — particularly beneficial for wearers whose tear film is disrupted by contact lenses, air conditioning, or screen use. 💻 Screen-Heavy Professionals High Dk/t and proactive tear support make INFUSE® One-Day one of the strongest performers for extended screen-based work where blink rate drops and comfort challenges mount. 👁️ Dry Eye Sufferers The phospholipid component of ProBalance Technology™ mirrors the lipid layer of the natural tear film — supporting evaporation control that is often compromised in dry eye conditions. 🏃 Active Lifestyles Daily disposables are the most hygienic and practical format for sport and active use. No cleaning, no cases — open, wear, and discard after each day. ✈️ Frequent Travellers No solution to pack, no case to lose. The 30-pack covers a month per eye — ideal for trips where carrying liquids through security is a hassle. 🌟 Comfort Upgraders If your current daily lens leaves you reaching for rewetting drops by mid-afternoon, ProBalance Technology™ offers a meaningfully different approach to sustained comfort. How to Use 1Wash your hands thoroughly with soap and water. Dry with a lint-free towel before handling any lens. 2Open the blister carefully — peel back the foil without squeezing the pack or spilling the storage solution. 3Check orientation — the lens should form a smooth bowl shape on your fingertip. If the edges flare outward, it is inside-out. Never insert an inside-out lens. 4Insert gently by holding lids open and placing the lens directly onto the cornea. Blink naturally to settle. Any discomfort or blurred vision? Remove, inspect, and reinsert. 5No solution needed. As a daily disposable, no lens case or cleaning solution is required. The blister solution can briefly keep the lens moist if you remove and reinsert during the day — it is not a cleaning agent. 6Remove before sleep unless your eye doctor has specifically  overnight wear. Remove before swimming, showering, or using a hot tub. 7Discard after one day. Pull the lower lid down, slide the lens down from the cornea, and pinch out gently. Never reuse a daily disposable lens. 💡 Screen use tip: Blink frequency drops significantly during screen work, reducing natural tear film replenishment. Apply the 20-20-20 rule — every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds. Ask your eye doctor about compatible lubricating drops if additional support is needed alongside your lenses. Why Shop at contactlens365.com? ✅ 100% Authentic Every product sourced exclusively from authorised distributors — zero risk of  or expired lenses. 🚀 Fast Dispatch Orders placed before cut-off ship same day. Track your parcel in real time from order to doorstep. 🛡️ Secure Payments , debit card, and other secure payment methods accepted. Your data is always protected. 💬 Expert Support Our customer care team is available 7 days a week to help with parameter selection, orders, and returns. 🔄 Auto-Reorder Set up a subscription and receive your lenses automatically every month — never run out again. ↩ Hassle-Free Returns Not satisfied? Our straightforward returns and exchange policy has you covered. Frequently Asked Questions Are INFUSE® One-Day and ULTRA® One Day the same lens? Yes. INFUSE® One-Day and ULTRA® One Day are the same lens sold under different brand names in different markets. Both use Kalifilcon A material and ProBalance Technology™ with identical specifications. If your  states "ULTRA One Day," the INFUSE® One-Day you receive is the same product. Do I need lens solution for the INFUSE® One-Day? No. As a daily disposable lens, no cleaning solution or storage case is required. Open a fresh blister each morning and discard the lens at the end of the day. What is ProBalance Technology™? ProBalance Technology™ is a proprietary blend of naturally inspired ingredients — including phospholipids and electrolytes — infused into the lens material. These ingredients are designed to work with the natural tear film rather than simply adding surface hydration, supporting both the lipid and aqueous layers of the tear film throughout the day. How long does one 30-pack last? A 30-lens box covers approximately one month for one eye. Two boxes — one per eye — provide a full month's supply for both eyes. Is the INFUSE® One-Day suitable for astigmatism? The standard spherical version does not correct astigmatism. The INFUSE® One-Day for Astigmatism (toric version) is available separately — please consult your eye care professional for the appropriate . Can I swim while wearing these lenses? No. All contact lenses should be removed before swimming, showering, or entering a hot tub. Water exposure — including tap water — substantially raises the risk of serious eye infection. What is the current price? For the latest pricing and any active promotions, visit the product page at contactlens365.com. Subscribe to our newsletter to receive exclusive  alerts. Can I sleep in INFUSE® One-Day lenses? Unless your eye doctor has specifically  extended wear, do not sleep in contact lenses. Engineered with a modern, eye-health-first formulation, it delivers high oxygen permeability, superior moisture retention, and exceptional comfort — from the first wear in the morning to the last moment at night. Order your ULTRA Multifocal at contactlens365.com with  authenticity,  expiry dates, and secure payment options. Developed through rigorous clinical research, the ULTRA Multifocal for Presbyopia is optimised for a wide range of prescriptions and visual needs. Users consistently report outstanding comfort and clarity during demanding activities such as extended screen work, driving, and reading — and satisfaction scores place it among the top-rated monthly multifocal lenses globally. At contactlens365.com we always stock the ULTRA Multifocal fresh from authorised distributors. 💡 Long screen hours, demanding visual tasks, or an active lifestyle can all challenge lens comfort. ULTRA Multifocal's MoistureSeal™ technology retains moisture for up to 16 hours — keeping your lenses comfortable and your vision clear throughout the day. Technical Specifications Parameter Value Material Samfilcon A (Silicone Hydrogel) Water Content 46% Base Curve (BC) 8.5 mm Diameter 14.2 mm Oxygen Transmissibility (Dk/t) 163 UV Protection None Power Range (SPH) –10.00 to +6.00 D ADD Options Low (+0.75–+1.50 D) / High (+1.75–+2.50 D) Replacement Schedule Monthly (30 days) Pack Contents 6 Lenses Manufacturer Bausch + Lomb Product Details The technical parameters of the ULTRA Multifocal for Presbyopia are designed to meet all standards required by modern eye science. The base curve and diameter are optimised to fit a broad range of corneal anatomies. With a Dk/t of 163, this lens leads the monthly multifocal category in oxygen transmissibility — ensuring the cornea stays healthy even during extended daily wear. Material choice is the foundation of comfort and safety. The 46% water content in Samfilcon A is carefully balanced: enough hydration for a natural on-eye feel, without drawing excessive moisture from the tear film. MoistureSeal® technology locks in this moisture for up to 16 hours of wear, independent of the lens care solution used. The power range of –10.00 to +6.00 D covers mild to high myopia and hyperopia, while the ADD options (Low and High) address the full clinical spectrum of presbyopia. At contactlens365.com we stock all available parameters — please consult your eye care professional for your exact  before ordering. ADD Parameter & Presbyopia Guide Multifocal lenses integrate multiple optical powers into a single lens, allowing simultaneous focus at distance, intermediate, and near ranges. The most important parameter — beyond the spherical power (SPH) — is the Addition (ADD) value. What Is the ADD Value? The ADD represents the extra plus power required on top of your distance correction to allow clear near vision. As the eye's natural lens loses flexibility with age, the required ADD increases. ADD values typically range from +0.75 D to +3.00 D and are measured by your eye care professional. Available ADD Options — ULTRA Multifocal LOW ADD +0.75 D — +1.50 D For early-stage presbyopia. Suitable for those who primarily notice difficulty with small print or prolonged near tasks. HIGH ADD +1.75 D — +2.50 D For moderate-to-advanced presbyopia. Recommended when both near and intermediate distances require significant correction. The choice between Low and High ADD is made by your eye care professional based on your current  and visual needs. Spherical Power (SPH) Range The ULTRA Multifocal is available from –10.00 to +6.00 D. Minus (–) values correct myopia (short-sightedness); plus (+) values correct hyperopia (long-sightedness). A power of 0.00 (plano) with an ADD value is used for pure presbyopia correction. Optical Design The ULTRA Multifocal uses a simultaneous vision — 3-Zone Progressive™ Design. Concentric optical zones are optimised for distance, intermediate, and near vision respectively. The brain naturally selects the sharpest image from both eyes through neural adaptation — a process that typically settles within one to two weeks. Technology Details Bausch + Lomb ULTRA® for Presbyopia balances three vision zones with its 3-Zone Progressive™ Design. The Samfilcon A material with 46% water content and Dk/t of 163 is among the highest oxygen-transmitting monthly multifocal lenses available — protecting corneal health for the full 30-day replacement cycle. MoistureSeal® technology maintains moisture loss below 5% over 16 hours of wear. Adaptation Period Switching to a multifocal lens is different from moving to a standard single-vision lens. During the first one to two weeks, the brain is learning to process the new optical system — mild visual fluctuation or a brief adjustment period is completely normal. To speed adaptation, wear your ULTRA Multifocal lenses for as long as possible each day and always use both eyes together. If discomfort or blurred vision persists beyond two weeks, consult your eye care professional. 📋  Guide Your multifocal lens  will show an ADD, Near Add, or Reading Add value alongside your SPH. Both right (OD) and left (OS) eyes will have separate SPH values but usually the same ADD. When ordering at contactlens365.com, enter each eye's values separately as shown on your . Who Is It For? 👓 Presbyopia Wearers (40+) Designed specifically for those who struggle to see both near and far — the hallmark of presbyopia, which typically begins after age 40. 📱 Reading Glass Avoiders Ideal for anyone who wants to read, use a phone, and drive — all without switching between glasses and lenses. 💻 Screen-Heavy Professionals The intermediate vision zone provides comfortable computer distance focus — essential for office workers and remote professionals. ✈️ Active Adults & Travellers One lens — all distances. No reading glasses to carry, lose, or juggle while on the move. How to Use & Care 1Wash your hands thoroughly with soap and water. Dry with a lint-free towel before handling any lens. 2Empty and refresh the lens case with fresh multipurpose solution before placing lenses inside — never top up old solution. 3Inspect the lens for any tears, discolouration, or deformation before insertion. Never use a damaged lens. 4Insert carefully by holding lids open and gently placing the lens on the cornea. Blink to settle, then check vision and comfort. 5Clean after removal — rub each lens surface with multipurpose solution for at least 5 seconds on each side, then rinse and store in fresh solution. Rubbing is recommended even with "no-rub" solutions. 6Replace every 30 days. Note the date when you open a new pack. Worn-out lenses accumulate protein deposits and increase infection risk — never exceed the replacement schedule. 7Remove before sleep unless your eye doctor has specifically  extended wear. Remove before swimming, showering, or using a hot tub. 8Maintain your case — rinse with solution (never tap water), wipe with a tissue, and air dry face-down daily. Replace the case every 1–3 months. Why Shop at contactlens365.com? ✅ 100% Authentic Every product sourced exclusively from authorised distributors — zero risk of  or expired lenses. 🚀 Fast Dispatch Orders placed before cut-off ship same day. Track your parcel in real time from order to doorstep. 🛡️ Secure Payments , debit card, and other secure payment methods accepted. Your data is always protected. 💬 Expert Support Our customer care team is available 7 days a week to help with ADD selection, orders, and returns. 🔄 Auto-Reorder Set up a subscription and receive your lenses automatically every month — never run out again. ↩ Hassle-Free Returns Not satisfied? Our straightforward returns and exchange policy has you covered. Frequently Asked Questions How should I store the ULTRA Multifocal lenses? After each use, clean the lenses with multipurpose contact lens solution and store them in a lens case filled with fresh solution. Never use tap water or saliva to rinse or store lenses. Where can I buy the Bausch + Lomb ULTRA Multifocal? You can order the ULTRA Multifocal for Presbyopia directly at contactlens365.com at competitive prices with secure payment and fast shipping. How long does one 6-lens box last? A 6-lens box covers approximately 3 months for one eye (one lens per month). Two boxes — one per eye — are typically required for 3 months of full daily wear. Is the ULTRA Multifocal suitable for astigmatism? The standard spherical version does not correct astigmatism. If you also have astigmatism, please consult your eye care professional — a toric multifocal alternative may be more appropriate for your . Can I swim while wearing these lenses? No. All contact lenses should be removed before swimming, showering, or entering a hot tub. Water exposure — including tap water — substantially raises the risk of serious eye infection. What is the current price of the ULTRA Multifocal? For the latest pricing and any active promotions, visit the ULTRA Multifocal product page at contactlens365.com. Subscribe to our newsletter to receive exclusive  alerts. How do I place an order at contactlens365.com? On the product page, select your base curve, power (SPH), and ADD value for each eye, then add to cart and complete the secure checkout. Your order will be dispatched promptly. Can I sleep in ULTRA Multifocal lenses? Unless your eye doctor has specifically  extended wear for this lens, do not sleep in contact lenses. } .hero-stats { grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); } .suited-grid, .why-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; } .tech-row { flex-direction: column; } } Alcon contactlens365.com TOTAL30®6 Pack Alcon's most advanced monthly lens — Water Gradient technology creates a virtually 100% water surface at the eye interface, delivering unmatched comfort from the first hour to the last. 6 lenses per box. ✦ Monthly Disposable 6 Lenses / Box Water Gradient Technology Lehfilcon A BC 8.4 mm · Dia 14.2 mm Monthly Wear Type 6 Lenses / Box 125 Dk/t O₂ Transmit. 55% Water Content ✓ 100% Genuine 📅 Expiry Date Checked 🚚 Fast Shipping 🔒 Secure Checkout ↩ Easy Returns Alcon TOTAL30® 6 Pack represents a breakthrough in monthly contact lens technology. Available at contactlens365.com, this is Alcon's flagship monthly lens — built around a genuinely novel material science that creates a lens surface behaving like pure water at the point it meets the eye. The result is a comfort experience that independent clinical studies describe as significantly better than other monthly lenses, even at the end of a long wearing day. Order at contactlens365.com with  authenticity,  expiry dates, and secure payment options. The TOTAL30® is not an incremental improvement on existing lens technology — it is a fundamentally different approach to how a silicone hydrogel lens is constructed. The Lehfilcon A material uses a Water Gradient design: the core of the lens is a high-performance silicone hydrogel for oxygen delivery, but the surface transitions progressively to a near-100% water composition. This means the part of the lens that actually contacts the cornea and eyelid has virtually no silicone — eliminating the surface friction and lipid deposit accumulation that causes discomfort in traditional silicone hydrogel lenses. 💧 Water Gradient technology creates a lens surface that is ~100% water at the eye interface — the only monthly lens engineered this way. It doesn't just add a water-based coating; the gradient is built into the material structure itself, so it doesn't wear off over the course of the day or the month. Technical Specifications Parameter Value Material Lehfilcon A (Silicone Hydrogel) Water Content 55% Surface Water Content ~100% (Water Gradient) Base Curve (BC) 8.4 mm Diameter 14.2 mm Oxygen Transmissibility (Dk/t) 125 UV Protection None Power Range (SPH) –12.00 to +8.00 D Replacement Schedule Monthly (30 days) Pack Contents 6 Lenses Manufacturer Alcon Key Technology Water Gradient A unique material architecture where water content increases progressively from the core of the lens to the surface — reaching ~100% water at the eye interface. This isn't a coating; it's built into the polymer structure, so it doesn't wear away with time or blinking. Lehfilcon A Alcon's proprietary silicone hydrogel polymer — developed specifically to enable the Water Gradient architecture. The silicone-rich core provides high oxygen transmissibility (Dk/t 125) while the water-rich surface minimises friction, deposit build-up, and lens awareness. Smart Surface™ The outermost layer of the TOTAL30® lens is a dense, highly lubricious hydrogel that resists protein and lipid deposits throughout the full 30-day replacement cycle — a key factor in maintaining consistent comfort week after week. Product Details Traditional silicone hydrogel lenses are excellent at oxygen delivery but have inherently hydrophobic (water-repelling) silicone at their surface. This attracts lipid deposits from the tear film and creates friction against the eyelid — both of which contribute to end-of-day discomfort. The TOTAL30® solves this by ensuring the surface the eye actually contacts is almost entirely water-based, while keeping the oxygen-delivering silicone core intact beneath it. The 55% bulk water content is higher than most silicone hydrogel monthly lenses, and the Dk/t of 125 remains comfortably above the 87 Dk/t threshold considered necessary for safe daily wear. The result is a lens that does not force a trade-off between oxygen performance and surface comfort — it delivers both simultaneously. The power range of –12.00 to +8.00 D covers an exceptionally wide  spectrum, from high myopia to moderate-to-strong hyperopia. The single base curve of 8.4 mm fits the majority of corneal geometries. At contactlens365.com, all available parameters are held in stock. Always confirm your current  with your eye care professional before ordering. Who Is It For? 👁️ Comfort-First Wearers If you've found other monthly lenses become uncomfortable by afternoon or evening, Water Gradient technology addresses the root cause — surface friction and deposit accumulation. 💻 Screen-Heavy Professionals The water-surface design maintains lubricity even during extended screen use when blinking is reduced — supporting tear film stability throughout demanding workdays. 🌬️ Dry or Challenging Environments Air-conditioned offices, aircraft cabins, and dry climates are particularly harsh on lens surfaces. The near-100% water surface is significantly more resilient to environmental drying. 🏃 Active Lifestyles High oxygen delivery and robust deposit resistance make TOTAL30® a reliable performer across a wide range of daily activities — from the office to the gym. 🔄 Monthly Lens Upgraders Already wearing a monthly lens but not fully satisfied? TOTAL30® is the most technically advanced monthly available — a meaningful step up from conventional silicone hydrogel monthlies. 📋 Wide  Range With SPH from –12.00 to +8.00 D, TOTAL30® serves high myopes and hyperopes who want a premium monthly lens and previously had limited options in this tier. How to Use & Care 1Wash your hands thoroughly with soap and water. Dry with a lint-free towel before handling any lens. 2Inspect before insertion — check for any tears, discolouration, or deformation. Verify orientation: a bowl shape is correct, flared edges mean inside-out. Never insert a damaged or inside-out lens. 3Insert gently by holding lids open and placing the lens onto the cornea. Blink to settle. If vision is blurred or comfort is poor after blinking, remove and recheck. 4Clean after every removal — rub each surface of the lens with multipurpose solution for at least 5 seconds per side, then rinse thoroughly. Rubbing is recommended even with "no-rub" labelled solutions. 5Store in fresh solution — always empty the lens case completely and refill with fresh multipurpose solution. Never top up old solution with new. 6Maintain your lens case — rinse daily with solution (never tap water), wipe with a clean tissue, and leave face-down to air dry. Replace the case every 1–3 months. 7Replace every 30 days — note the date when you open a new lens. Worn-out lenses accumulate deposits and increase infection risk regardless of how well they are cleaned. 8Remove before sleep unless your eye doctor has specifically  overnight wear. Always remove before swimming, showering, or using a hot tub. 💡 Deposit tip: The Smart Surface™ of the TOTAL30® is highly deposit-resistant, but weekly enzymatic cleaning (if recommended by your eye doctor) can extend consistent comfort across the full replacement cycle — especially useful for wearers with protein-heavy tear chemistry. Why Shop at contactlens365.com? ✅ 100% Authentic Every product sourced exclusively from authorised distributors — zero risk of  or expired lenses. 🚀 Fast Dispatch Orders placed before cut-off ship same day. Track your parcel in real time from order to doorstep. 🛡️ Secure Payments , debit card, and other secure payment methods accepted. Your data is always protected. 💬 Expert Support Our customer care team is available 7 days a week to help with parameter selection, orders, and returns. 🔄 Auto-Reorder Set up a subscription and receive your TOTAL30® automatically every 3 months — never run out again. ↩ Hassle-Free Returns Not satisfied? Our straightforward returns and exchange policy has you covered. Frequently Asked Questions How should I store the TOTAL30® lenses? After each use, clean the lenses with multipurpose contact lens solution and store them in a clean lens case filled with fresh solution. Never use tap water, saliva, or saline solution for storage. What makes the TOTAL30® different from other monthly lenses? The TOTAL30® uses Water Gradient technology — a material architecture where the lens surface transitions to ~100% water content at the eye interface. No other monthly lens is built this way. The surface behaves like water rather than silicone, which dramatically reduces friction, deposit attraction, and end-of-day lens awareness. How long does one 6-pack last? A 6-lens box covers approximately 3 months for one eye (one lens per month). Two boxes — one per eye — are required for 3 months of full daily wear in both eyes. Is the TOTAL30® suitable for astigmatism? The standard spherical version does not correct astigmatism. The TOTAL30® for Astigmatism (toric version) is available separately — please consult your eye care professional for the appropriate . Can I swim while wearing these lenses? No. All contact lenses should be removed before swimming, showering, or entering a hot tub. Water exposure — including tap water — substantially raises the risk of serious eye infection. What multipurpose solution should I use with TOTAL30®? The TOTAL30® is compatible with all major multipurpose solutions. Your eye care professional may have a specific recommendation based on your tear chemistry and lens care routine. Always follow the solution manufacturer's instructions alongside your lens care guidance. What is the current price of the TOTAL30® 6 Pack? For the latest pricing and any active promotions, visit the product page at contactlens365.com. Subscribe to our newsletter to receive exclusive  alerts. Can I sleep in TOTAL30® lenses? Unless your eye doctor has specifically  extended wear, do not sleep in contact lenses. Developed by Bausch + Lomb through rigorous clinical research, the ULTRA One Day is optimised for comfort across a full day of wear — whether that means 8 hours at a desk or 16 hours on the go. With a Dk/t of 163, it delivers class-leading oxygen transmissibility for a daily lens, supporting long-term corneal health even with demanding daily use. The 90-pack format means fewer reorders and better value — covering a full 3-month supply per eye. 💧 MoistureSeal™ technology retains moisture throughout the day without relying on added wetting agents that wash away over time — the same principle that made the ULTRA Monthly one of the best-rated lenses for comfort, now in a daily format. Technical Specifications Parameter Value Material Samfilcon A (Silicone Hydrogel) Water Content 46% Base Curve (BC) 8.5 mm Diameter 14.2 mm Oxygen Transmissibility (Dk/t) 163 UV Protection None Power Range (SPH) –12.00 to +6.00 D Replacement Schedule Daily Disposable Pack Contents 90 Lenses Manufacturer Bausch + Lomb Key Technology MoistureSeal™ Bausch + Lomb's proprietary moisture-retention technology locks hydration into the lens matrix itself — not just a surface coating — so comfort levels remain consistent from the first hour of wear to the last, without depending on rewetting drops. Samfilcon A The same high-performance silicone hydrogel material used in the ULTRA Monthly lens. With 46% water content and Dk/t 163, it balances outstanding oxygen permeability with natural on-eye feel — one of the highest Dk/t values in any daily lens. Product Details The ULTRA One Day 90 Pack uses Samfilcon A — the same silicone hydrogel polymer found in the ULTRA Monthly — giving daily wearers access to the same proven material without a monthly replacement schedule. The 46% water content sits at an optimal balance point: sufficient hydration for a comfortable, natural feel, without drawing moisture excessively from the tear film over the course of the day. At Dk/t 163, the ULTRA One Day has one of the highest oxygen transmissibility values of any daily disposable lens on the market. This matters for corneal health: adequate oxygen supply reduces the risk of corneal oedema, neovascularisation, and the discomfort that comes from oxygen-deprived wear — particularly relevant for those wearing lenses for 12 or more hours. The power range of –12.00 to +6.00 D covers a wide spectrum from high myopia to moderate hyperopia, making the ULTRA One Day 90 Pack accessible to a broad range of wearers. At contactlens365.com, all available parameters are held in stock for prompt dispatch. Always confirm your current  with your eye care professional before ordering. Who Is It For? 👁️ Dry Eye Sufferers MoistureSeal™ technology provides sustained hydration without wetting agents that deplete over time — a meaningful difference for those prone to end-of-day dryness. 💻 Screen-Heavy Professionals High oxygen transmissibility and sustained moisture make the ULTRA One Day well-suited to extended desk work and screen-intensive environments. 🏃 Active Lifestyles Daily disposables are the most hygienic and convenient format for sport and active use — open a fresh lens and discard after each session. ✈️ Frequent Travellers The 90-pack covers 3 months per eye. No solution to carry through airport security — as low-maintenance as daily wear gets. 🔄 ULTRA Monthly Wearers Already love the ULTRA Monthly? The ULTRA One Day uses the same Samfilcon A material and MoistureSeal™ technology in a daily format — familiar comfort, zero maintenance. 🌟 First-Time Lens Wearers No cleaning routine, no storage case, no solution. Daily disposables are the easiest introduction to contact lenses — and the ULTRA One Day is one of the most comfortable options available. How to Use 1Wash your hands thoroughly with soap and water. Dry with a lint-free towel before handling any lens. 2Open the blister carefully — peel back the foil without squeezing the pack or spilling the storage solution. 3Check orientation — place the lens on your fingertip. A correctly oriented lens forms a smooth bowl shape. If the edges flare outward like a saucer, it is inside-out. Never insert an inside-out lens. 4Insert the lens by holding your upper and lower lids wide open, gently placing the lens onto the cornea, then releasing your lids. Blink gently to settle. 5Check comfort and vision. Any discomfort or blurred vision? Remove, inspect for damage, and reinsert. Persistent issues — stop wearing and consult your eye doctor. 6No solution needed. As a daily disposable, no lens case or cleaning solution is required. If you briefly remove the lens during the day, the remaining blister solution can keep it moist — do not use it to clean the lens. 7Remove before sleep unless your eye doctor has specifically  overnight wear. Remove before swimming, showering, or using a hot tub. 8Discard after one day. Pull the lower lid down, slide the lens downward from the cornea, and pinch out gently. Never reuse a daily disposable lens. 💡 Screen use tip: Blink frequency drops significantly during screen work, reducing natural tear film replenishment. Apply the 20-20-20 rule — every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds. Ask your eye doctor about compatible lubricating drops if needed. Our straightforward returns and exchange policy has you covered. Frequently Asked Questions Do I need lens solution for the ULTRA One Day 90 Pack? No. As a daily disposable lens, no cleaning solution or storage case is required. Simply open a fresh blister each morning and discard the lens at the end of the day. How is the ULTRA One Day different from the ULTRA Monthly? Both use the same Samfilcon A material and MoistureSeal™ technology. The key difference is the replacement schedule: the ULTRA One Day is discarded after each day of wear, requiring no cleaning or storage. The ULTRA Monthly is worn for up to 30 days with daily cleaning and storage. How long does one 90-pack last? A 90-lens box covers approximately 3 months for one eye. Two boxes — one per eye — provide a full 3-month supply for both eyes. Is the ULTRA One Day suitable for astigmatism? The standard spherical version does not correct astigmatism. If you have astigmatism, please consult your eye care professional — a toric daily lens may be more appropriate for your . Can I swim while wearing these lenses? No. All contact lenses should be removed before swimming, showering, or entering a hot tub. Water exposure — including tap water — substantially raises the risk of serious eye infection. Technical Specifications Parameter Value Material Senofilcon A (Silicone Hydrogel) Water Content 38% Base Curve (BC) 8.4 mm and 8.8 mm Diameter 14.3 mm Oxygen Transmissibility (Dk/t) 121 UV Protection Class 1 — UVA 99.9%, UVB 100% Blue-Violet Light Filter OptiBlue™ (filters ~400–450 nm) Power Range (SPH) –12.00 to +8.00 D Replacement Schedule Daily Disposable Pack Contents 90 Lenses Manufacturer Johnson & Johnson Vision Key Technologies TearStable™ Stabilises the tear film on the lens surface for sustained optical clarity and comfort — especially effective during prolonged screen use when blink rate naturally decreases. OptiBlue™ A built-in blue-violet light filter (approximately 400–450 nm) designed to reduce exposure from digital screens and artificial lighting without distorting colour perception. Class 1 UV Blocks 99.9% of UVA and 100% of UVB rays — the highest UV protection class available in any contact lens, providing meaningful daily defence against solar radiation. Product Details The technical parameters of the ACUVUE® OASYS MAX 1-Day 90 Pack are designed to meet every standard required by modern eye science. With two base curve options (8.4 mm and 8.8 mm) and a 14.3 mm diameter, the lens accommodates a broad range of corneal geometries for a secure, comfortable fit. At Dk/t 121, oxygen transmissibility is well above the threshold for healthy extended daily wear. The 38% water content in Senofilcon A is precisely calibrated — enough hydration for a natural on-eye feel, without drawing excessive moisture from the tear film. TearStable™ technology builds on this to actively stabilise the lens-tear interface, delivering consistent visual performance throughout the day. The power range of –12.00 to +8.00 D is one of the widest available in a daily lens, making the ACUVUE® OASYS MAX 1-Day 90 Pack suitable for mild to high myopia as well as hyperopia. Designed for anyone seeking a natural yet striking eye color transformation, Air Optix COLORS combines advanced color pigmentation, a comfortable fit, and eye-friendly silicone hydrogel material — making it an ideal choice both aesthetically and practically. Air Optix COLORS features a specially developed color layer crafted in collaboration with optical designers and eye health professionals. This layer is designed to change your eye color without disrupting the natural tissue of the eye, while allowing the cornea to breathe freely. Even in bright sunlight, the color appears completely natural — enhancing the depth and brightness of your eyes rather than looking artificial. Technical Specifications Specification Value Material Lotrafilcon B (Silicone Hydrogel) Water Content 33% Base Curve (BC) 8.6 mm Diameter 14.2 mm Oxygen Transmissibility (Dk/t) 138 UV Protection None Power Range -10.00 to +6.00 D (Plano also available) Wear Schedule Monthly (30 days) Pack Contents 6 Lenses Manufacturer Alcon Color Options Color Name Shade Pure Hazel Hazel Brown Brown Honey Honey Blue Blue Brilliant Blue Bright Blue True Sapphire Sapphire Blue Green Green Gemstone Green Emerald Green Gray Gray Sterling Gray Silver Gray Amethyst Amethyst Purple Turquoise Turquoise Detailed Information About Air Optix COLORS 6 Pack The technical parameters of Air Optix COLORS are designed to meet all standards required by modern optometry. The base curve (BC) and diameter values are optimized to accommodate a wide range of eye anatomies. Oxygen transmissibility (Dk/t) is one of the most critical parameters for keeping the cornea healthy by allowing CO₂ to escape from beneath the lens — and Air Optix COLORS performs well above industry averages in this regard. Material choice is the fundamental factor that determines the balance between comfort and safety in Air Optix COLORS. Water content plays a key role in compatibility with the tear film: too high a water content draws moisture from the tear film causing dryness, while too low reduces comfort. The water content of Air Optix COLORS is optimized with this balance in mind. Note that while the lens does not include UV protection, wearing UV-blocking sunglasses outdoors is always recommended for full eye protection. The power range of Air Optix COLORS covers a broad spectrum from mild myopia to high myopia and hyperopia — and a plano (0.00) option is also available for those who do not require vision correction but simply want to change their eye color. This makes Air Optix COLORS 6 Pack suitable for virtually every user. At lenssepeti.net, we maintain stock across all available parameters and fulfill your orders as quickly as possible. If you are unsure of your  values, consult your eye care professional for an up-to-date  before ordering. Air Optix COLORS delivers consistent optical clarity thanks to extensive quality testing during manufacturing. The lens centration capability ensures sharp vision across different gaze directions and eye movements throughout the day. Users of Air Optix COLORS consistently report superior comfort and clarity during demanding visual activities such as extended screen use, driving, and sports. At lenssepeti.net, Air Optix COLORS 6 Pack is always supplied in its original blister packaging with  expiry dates. Wearing & Care Instructions Before inserting Air Optix COLORS, wash your hands thoroughly with soap and water, rub well, and dry with a lint-free towel. Before removing a lens from its case, discard the old solution and replace it with fresh solution — this maintains cleanliness and comfort. Place the lens on your fingertip and check the orientation, then inspect for any tears, discoloration, or deformation before inserting. To insert the lens, use your thumb and middle finger to hold your eyelids open, then gently place Air Optix COLORS onto the cornea using your index finger. Release your eyelid and blink a few times to confirm the lens is seated correctly. If you experience blurred vision or a foreign body sensation, remove the lens, clean it, and reinsert. If the problem persists, discontinue use and consult your eye doctor. Remove Air Optix COLORS every night before sleeping — do not sleep in lenses unless specifically directed by your eye care professional. After removal, clean both surfaces with a multipurpose lens solution by rubbing for at least 5 seconds, rinse thoroughly, and store in a lens case filled with fresh solution. The rubbing step is strongly recommended even if the solution is labeled "no rub," as mechanical cleaning significantly reduces microbial contamination. Replace Air Optix COLORS monthly and never exceed the manufacturer's recommended replacement schedule — worn-out lenses increase the risk of protein deposits, eye infections, and discomfort. Note the date when you open a new pack and track your schedule. At lenssepeti.net, you can set up automatic monthly orders for Air Optix COLORS 6 Pack so you always have a fresh supply ready without worrying about running out. Refill your lens case with fresh solution daily — never top off old solution, always empty and refill completely. Clean your lens case with multipurpose solution once a week, invert it, and allow it to air dry on a paper towel. Never clean your case with tap water or saliva. Replace your lens case every 1–3 months. Do not wear Air Optix COLORS while swimming, showering, or in a sauna — water exposure significantly increases the risk of microorganisms on the lens. Who Is It Suitable For? Air Optix COLORS 6 Pack is designed for anyone who wants to add a new dimension to their appearance by changing their eye color. The plano (0.00) option means users without any vision correction needs can also wear Air Optix COLORS purely for cosmetic purposes. Engineered with Johnson & Johnson Vision's most advanced technologies, it delivers precise, consistent vision correction for astigmatism — without sacrificing the all-day comfort that the OASYS MAX platform is known for. Order at contactlens365.com with  authenticity,  expiry dates, and secure payment options. Astigmatism occurs when the cornea or lens has an irregular curvature, causing blurred or distorted vision at all distances. Correcting it with a contact lens requires a toric design — one that incorporates both a spherical power and a cylindrical correction at a specific axis, and crucially, stays precisely oriented on the eye throughout the day. The ACUVUE® OASYS MAX 1-Day for Astigmatism achieves this through BLINK STABILIZED® Design, keeping the lens correctly positioned with every blink, head movement, and change of gaze direction. ✦ ACUVUE® OASYS MAX 1-Day for Astigmatism is the only daily toric lens combining BLINK STABILIZED® orientation stability, TearStable™ moisture technology, OptiBlue™ blue-violet light filtering, and Class 1 UV protection — an unmatched combination for astigmatism wearers. Technical Specifications Parameter Value Material Senofilcon A (Silicone Hydrogel) Water Content 38% Base Curve (BC) 8.4 mm Diameter 14.3 mm Oxygen Transmissibility (Dk/t) 121 UV Protection Class 1 — UVA 99.9%, UVB 100% Blue-Violet Light Filter OptiBlue™ (~400–450 nm) Stabilisation Design BLINK STABILIZED® Design Sphere (SPH) Range –9.00 to +4.00 D Cylinder (CYL) Range –0.75, –1.25, –1.75, –2.25 D Axis Range 10° – 180° (in 10° steps) Replacement Schedule Daily Disposable Pack Contents 30 Lenses Manufacturer Johnson & Johnson Vision Key Technologies BLINK STABILIZED® Design Four stabilisation zones interact with the natural blink to keep the lens correctly oriented at all times — ensuring consistent cylindrical correction regardless of eye movement or gaze direction. TearStable™ Stabilises the tear film on the lens surface for sustained optical clarity and comfort — particularly effective during prolonged screen use when blink rate naturally decreases. OptiBlue™ A built-in blue-violet light filter (~400–450 nm) designed to reduce exposure from digital screens and artificial lighting without affecting colour perception. Class 1 UV Blocks 99.9% of UVA and 100% of UVB rays — the highest UV protection class available in any contact lens, providing meaningful daily solar defence. Understanding Your Toric  A toric lens  contains three parameters that a standard spherical lens  does not. Before ordering, confirm all three values with your eye care professional. SPH — Sphere Distance / Near Power The standard refractive correction for myopia (–) or hyperopia (+). Range for this lens: –9.00 to +4.00 D. A value of 0.00 (plano) is also available. CYL — Cylinder Astigmatism Power The power needed to correct the irregular curvature causing astigmatism. Available values: –0.75, –1.25, –1.75, –2.25 D. Always a minus value in contact lens prescriptions. AXIS Orientation Angle The angle (in degrees) at which the cylindrical correction must be positioned. Range: 10° to 180° in 10° steps. This is what BLINK STABILIZED® Design maintains on your eye. BC & DIA Fit Parameters Base curve 8.4 mm and diameter 14.3 mm — fixed for this lens. Your eye doctor confirms these are suitable for your corneal geometry. 📋 How to Read Your Toric  Your  will show values for both eyes — right eye as OD, left eye as OS. Each eye will have its own SPH, CYL, and AXIS values. When ordering at contactlens365.com, enter each eye's parameters separately exactly as written on your . If your CYL value or axis does not match an available option, your eye doctor will advise the closest appropriate substitute. What Is Astigmatism? Astigmatism is one of the most common refractive conditions, affecting roughly one in three people to some degree. It occurs when the cornea or the natural lens of the eye has an oblong rather than perfectly spherical shape — like a rugby ball rather than a football. This causes light entering the eye to focus at two different points instead of one, resulting in blurred or distorted vision at all distances. Unlike myopia or hyperopia, astigmatism cannot be corrected with a standard spherical lens — a toric lens is required. Why Stability Matters in a Toric Lens For a toric lens to correct astigmatism accurately, it must stay orientated at precisely the prescribed axis angle throughout the day. Any rotation — even a few degrees — will reduce the quality of vision correction. BLINK STABILIZED® Design uses four carefully engineered stabilisation zones that work with the natural blink action to consistently re-orient the lens, making it one of the most reliably stable daily toric designs available. Who Is It For? 👁️ Astigmatism Wearers Purpose-built for astigmatism correction with a precise toric optical design and BLINK STABILIZED® orientation — sharp, consistent vision all day. 💻 Screen-Heavy Professionals TearStable™ and OptiBlue™ work together to reduce digital eye fatigue — ideal for anyone spending long hours in front of screens. 🏃 Active & Sporty Lifestyles BLINK STABILIZED® Design keeps the lens correctly positioned even during physical activity, head movements, and direction changes. ✈️ Travellers No solution, no case, no cleaning routine. Daily disposables are the most travel-friendly lens format — open, wear, and discard. ☀️ UV-Conscious Wearers Class 1 UV protection provides the maximum UV blocking available in a contact lens — every day, without thinking about it. 🔄 Previous Toric Lens Switchers If you have experienced rotation, discomfort, or inconsistent vision in other toric lenses, the OASYS MAX platform is worth trialling with your eye doctor. How to Use 1Wash your hands thoroughly with soap and water. Dry with a lint-free towel before handling any lens. 2Open the blister carefully — peel back the foil without squeezing the pack or spilling the storage solution. 3Check orientation — place the lens on your fingertip. It should form a smooth bowl shape. If the edges flare outward, it is inside-out. Never insert an inside-out toric lens — the stabilisation zones will not function correctly. 4Insert gently by holding lids open and placing the lens directly onto the cornea. Blink naturally — BLINK STABILIZED® Design will settle the lens into its correct axis position within a few blinks. 5Allow a moment to settle. Toric lenses may take a blink or two longer than spherical lenses to orientate after insertion. If vision is blurred after several blinks, remove, inspect, and reinsert. 6No solution needed. As a daily disposable, no lens case or cleaning solution is required. The blister solution can briefly keep the lens moist if you need to remove and reinsert during the day — but it is not a cleaning agent. 7Remove before sleep unless your eye doctor has specifically  overnight wear. Remove before swimming, showering, or using a hot tub. 8Discard after one day. Pull the lower lid down, slide the lens downward from the cornea, and pinch out gently. Never reuse a daily disposable toric lens. 💡 Screen use tip: Blink frequency drops during screen work, which can reduce tear film stability and temporarily affect toric lens orientation. Apply the 20-20-20 rule — every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds. Ask your eye doctor about compatible lubricating drops if needed. Track your parcel in real time from order to doorstep. 🛡️ Secure Payments , debit card, and other secure payment methods accepted. Your data is always protected. 💬 Expert Support Our team is available 7 days a week to help with toric parameter selection, CYL/axis queries, orders, and returns. 🔄 Auto-Reorder Set up a subscription and receive your lenses automatically every month — never run out again. ↩ Hassle-Free Returns Not satisfied? Our straightforward returns and exchange policy has you covered. Frequently Asked Questions Do I need lens solution for the ACUVUE® OASYS MAX 1-Day for Astigmatism? No. As a daily disposable lens, no cleaning solution or storage case is required. Open a fresh blister each morning and discard the lens at the end of the day. How is a toric lens  different from a regular one? A toric  includes three additional parameters: Cylinder (CYL) for the astigmatism power, Axis for the orientation angle, and the standard Sphere (SPH) for distance correction. All three values are needed to order correctly — check your  carefully. How long does one 30-pack last? A 30-lens box covers approximately one month for one eye. Two boxes — one per eye — are required for a full month of daily wear in both eyes. What if my cylinder or axis value isn't listed? The available CYL values are –0.75, –1.25, –1.75, and –2.25 D, with axis from 10° to 180° in 10° steps. If your  falls between these values, your eye doctor will recommend the best match. Never self-substitute — always follow professional guidance. Will the toric lens rotate on my eye? BLINK STABILIZED® Design is engineered specifically to prevent unwanted rotation. Four stabilisation zones interact with the eyelid during each blink to consistently re-orient the lens to its correct axis position — even during physical activity or changes in gaze direction. Can I swim while wearing these lenses? No. All contact lenses should be removed before swimming, showering, or entering a hot tub. Water exposure — including tap water — substantially raises the risk of serious eye infection. What is the current price? For the latest pricing and active promotions, visit the product page at contactlens365.com. Subscribe to our newsletter to receive exclusive  alerts. Can I sleep in ACUVUE® OASYS MAX 1-Day for Astigmatism lenses? Unless your eye doctor has specifically  extended wear, do not sleep in contact lenses. } .quick-nav ul { display: flex; gap: 30px; list-style: none; flex-wrap: wrap; } .quick-nav ul li a { font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; color: #1B2F6E; text-decoration: none; padding: 4px 0; border-bottom: 2px solid transparent; transition: border-color 0.2s; } .quick-nav ul li a:hover { border-bottom-color: #e8922a; color: #e8922a; } /* SECTIONS */ section { padding: 60px 0; } section:nth-child(even) { background: #f9fafb; } h2 { font-size: 28px; font-weight: 800; color: #1B2F6E; margin-bottom: 18px; line-height: 1.3; } h2 span { color: #e8922a; } h3 { font-size: 20px; font-weight: 700; color: #1B2F6E; margin-bottom: 10px; } p { margin-bottom: 14px; color: #333; } strong { color: #1B2F6E; } /* LENS TYPE CARDS — 4 büyük kart */ .lens-type-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(240px, 1fr)); gap: 24px; margin-top: 34px; } .lens-type-card { border-radius: 14px; padding: 32px 24px; text-align: center; position: relative; overflow: hidden; transition: transform 0.2s, box-shadow 0.2s; text-decoration: none; display: block; color: inherit; } .lens-type-card:hover { transform: translateY(-5px); box-shadow: 0 12px 30px rgba(27,47,110,0.18); color: inherit; text-decoration: none; } .lens-type-card.daily { background: linear-gradient(135deg, #e8f4ff, #d0e8ff); border: 2px solid #a8d0f8; } .lens-type-card.weekly { background: linear-gradient(135deg, #e8fff2, #d0f0e0); border: 2px solid #8ad4b0; } .lens-type-card.monthly { background: linear-gradient(135deg, #fff5e0, #ffe8c0); border: 2px solid #f0c060; } .lens-type-card.colored { background: linear-gradient(135deg, #f5e8ff, #e8d0ff); border: 2px solid #c8a0f0; } .lens-type-icon { font-size: 42px; margin-bottom: 14px; display: block; } .lens-type-card h3 { font-size: 18px; margin-bottom: 10px; } .lens-type-card p { font-size: 13.5px; color: #444; margin-bottom: 16px; line-height: 1.6; } .lens-type-card.daily h3 { color: #1a4a8a; } .lens-type-card.weekly h3 { color: #1a6a4a; } .lens-type-card.monthly h3 { color: #8a5a10; } .lens-type-card.colored h3 { color: #5a1a8a; } .lens-btn { display: inline-block; padding: 9px 20px; border-radius: 20px; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 700; text-decoration: none; transition: opacity 0.2s; } .lens-btn:hover { opacity: 0.85; } .lens-type-card.daily .lens-btn { background: #1a4a8a; color: #fff; } .lens-type-card.weekly .lens-btn { background: #1a6a4a; color: #fff; } .lens-type-card.monthly .lens-btn { background: #8a5a10; color: #fff; } .lens-type-card.colored .lens-btn { background: #5a1a8a; color: #fff; } /* BENEFITS GRID */ .benefits-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(300px, 1fr)); gap: 20px; margin-top: 30px; } .benefit-card { background: #fff; border: 1px solid #e0e8ff; border-radius: 10px; padding: 22px 24px; display: flex; gap: 14px; align-items: flex-start; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(27,47,110,0.06); transition: box-shadow 0.2s; } .benefit-card:hover { box-shadow: 0 4px 18px rgba(27,47,110,0.12); } .benefit-icon { font-size: 22px; flex-shrink: 0; margin-top: 2px; } .benefit-card h3 { font-size: 16px; margin-bottom: 6px; color: #1B2F6E; } .benefit-card p { font-size: 14px; margin: 0; color: #555; } /* HOW TO ORDER */ .steps { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(220px, 1fr)); gap: 24px; margin-top: 30px; } .step-card { text-align: center; padding: 30px 20px; background: #fff; border-radius: 12px; border: 1px solid #e0e8ff; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(27,47,110,0.05); } .step-number { width: 48px; height: 48px; background: #1B2F6E; color: #e8c96a; font-size: 22px; font-weight: 900; border-radius: 50%; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; margin: 0 auto 16px; } .step-card h3 { font-size: 17px; margin-bottom: 8px; } .step-card p { font-size: 14px; color: #555; margin: 0; } /* BRANDS */ .brands-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(480px, 1fr)); gap: 24px; margin-top: 30px; } .brand-card { background: #fff; border-radius: 12px; border: 1px solid #e0e8ff; padding: 24px 28px; box-shadow: 0 2px 10px rgba(27,47,110,0.06); transition: transform 0.2s, box-shadow 0.2s; } .brand-card:hover { transform: translateY(-3px); box-shadow: 0 6px 20px rgba(27,47,110,0.12); } .brand-card h3 { font-size: 18px; margin-bottom: 10px; color: #1B2F6E;
